## Skill inventory & UNIX-philosophy audit

Audit of which skills run at each phase, split by invoker, to check the design decision 10 toolkit-symlink premise still holds (every skill callable from both contexts).

Legend: `[E]` exists today · `[R]` exists, refined for narrower focus · `[N]` new, must be authored · `[—]` not a skill (CLI / built-in / scaffolding).

### The framing this audit checks against

Per operator review (c_b76ab7), the working split is:

- **Human** drives the **why** (initiative goal + KRs) and the **what** (the outcome-focused Linear issue — agent-assisted, but human-led).
- **Agents** handle the **how** — implementation, verification, PR preparation, feedback response — via a specific set of skills/workflows the human defines.
- The **how** can be invoked manually (operator at the keyboard) OR automatically (drain-cycle running the same skills in a worker session). The skills are the same primitives in both cases.

The audit below splits skills by *invocation context* (operator vs worker), and within the worker context shows how each skill maps to a `how`-step the human predefined.

### Skills *you* invoke during operator-side planning & manual work

| Phase | Skill | Status | What it does |
|---|---|---|---|
| Initiative shape | `/shape:initiative` | [E] | Six-field initiative gate + 11-rule rubric. |
| Idea triage | `/idea-triage` | [E] | Frames new problems before they become tickets. |
| Plan review | `/shape:plan-review` | [E] | Evaluates an existing plan against a rubric. |
| Task breakdown | `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` | [E] | Decomposes a ticket/spec into sub-tasks. |
| Spec writing | `/spec-driven-development` | [E] | Requirements + ACs before code. |
| Design doc | `/shape:design-doc` | [E] | Larger-than-ticket architecture artefact. |
| Doc / PR review | `/crit:crit` | [E] | Inline review of docs, plans, branch diffs, live URLs. |
| ADR | `/documentation-and-adrs` | [E] | Captures architectural decisions. |
| Code review (manual) | `/code-review-and-quality` | [E] | Five-axis review of branch/PR diff. |
| Debugging | `/debugging-and-error-recovery` | [E] | Reproduce → localize → fix → guard. |
| Implementation (manual) | `/incremental-implementation`, `/test-driven-development`, `/source-driven-development`, `/git-workflow-and-versioning`, `/shipping-and-launch` | [E] | Standard build/ship chain when you do the work yourself. |
| Ticket assembly for drain queue | `/spec-driven-development` + `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` | [E] | Two-skill combo that produces a drain-ready ticket. |

### Skills the *drain-cycle worker* invokes per ticket

| Role / Phase | Skill | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **1. Pre-Implementation Reviewer** — sizing + AC sufficiency + breakdown | `/shape:outcome-tasks` *(refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`)* | **[R]** | Refines the existing task-breakdown skill to focus on one outcome-focused task (typically a Linear issue). Inputs: the issue body + its ACs. Outputs: enriched AC checklist, sizing/stack-shape decision (1 stack vs N), per-stack implementation task list. Pairs symmetrically with `/shape:outcome-verify` (row 3). `/shape:plan-review` is the wrong fit — it operates on whole design docs, not single outcome-focused tasks (c_b76ab7). |
| **2a. Implementer** — code-writing pass | `/incremental-implementation` | [E] | Worker's main loop. |
| **2b. Implementer** — proof of behaviour | `/test-driven-development` | [E] | Runs alongside 2a. |
| **2c. Implementer** — doc-verified API use | `/source-driven-development` | [E] | When touching external libs. |
| **2d. Implementer** — code-review sub-agent (in-session) | `/code-review-and-quality` | [E] | Already mandated by `linear-workflow.md` as pre-Done step in worker. |
| **2e. Implementer** — commits | `/git-workflow-and-versioning` | [E] | Stack-aware via Graphite MCP. |
| **2f. Implementer** — error recovery | `/debugging-and-error-recovery` | [E] | When tests fail mid-session. |
| **3. Outcome Verifier** — spec-adherence / outcome | new skill — working name `/shape:outcome-verify` | **[N]** | Genuinely new: takes ticket body + diff + run-log, asks "did this build what was specified and did the outcome land," writes structured verdict to run log. Distinct from `/code-review-and-quality` in input (spec + diff, not just diff) and output (verdict for run-log, not five-axis review). Pairs symmetrically with `/shape:outcome-tasks` (row 1) — one defines the outcome-shaped task list, the other verifies the outcome was delivered. |
| **4. Pre-Human-Review Preparer** — PR body + auto-merge route | new skill — working name `/shape:pr-prepare` | **[N]** | Reads stack PRs from Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body; tags auto-merge eligible vs human-review-needed. |
| **5. PR-Feedback Responder** — address review comments | new skill — working name `/shape:pr-respond` | **[N]** | Reads PR review threads via Graphite MCP, makes the fix in the right worktree, pushes to stack. Trigger is drain-cycle-side (polling / Action / one-keystroke), but the skill itself is the same primitive regardless of trigger. |
| **6. Grader** (auto-draft) | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | **[—]** | Per operator review (c_832b0f): this is a CLI command on drain-cycle, not a skill. One-shot internal prompt: ticket + run-log + diff → fills the grade template with `status: draft`. Operator can invoke it manually on past tickets to retro-fill grades. |

### Non-skill primitives the worker still uses

Included so the audit is complete — these aren't skills but are the rest of the composition surface.

| Primitive | What it is | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*` | Linear MCP — read/write issues, comments, status, labels, cycles | The work-queue interface. |
| `mcp__graphite__*` | Graphite MCP — manage stacked PRs | The PR-side interface. |
| `mcp__honeycomb__*` | Honeycomb MCP — observability | Operability surface; OTel spans already ship since `3eae2f5`. |
| `drain-cycle` CLI | Dispatcher: worktree creation, worker spawning, run-log writing, grader invocation | Orchestration, not a skill. |
| `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` | New subcommand — drafts the per-ticket grade template after a drain finishes (or retro on a past issue) | Per c_832b0f, replaces what was previously sketched as `/shape:grade-draft`. |
| `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` | KR-grading subcommand — reads confirmed grade templates, reports pass-rate, warns about un-confirmed drafts | Carried over from round-3 doc. |
| `gh` / `gt` CLIs | GitHub + Graphite shells | Called from inside skills (e.g. `/git-workflow-and-versioning`). |

### UNIX-philosophy audit — findings

**Strengths:**

1. **Shared toolkit confirmed.** Every skill in the worker table is also invokable by you manually — decision 10's symlink working as intended. The four `[N]` skills inherit this property because they'll be authored as standard skills, not drain-cycle-internal logic.
2. **Each skill is single-purpose.** No skill in either table does two unrelated things; the role-to-skill mapping is roughly 1:1, and where it's many-to-one (e.g. Implementer using five skills sequentially) it's because the *role* spans multiple phases, not because a skill is overloaded.
3. **Reuse over new authoring where defensible.** Five of the six worker roles use existing skills; only three slots need genuinely new skills (plus one borderline).

**Warnings:**

1. **`/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` refinement scope.** Refining the existing skill to focus on a single outcome-focused task is conceptually clean (c_b76ab7), but the existing skill is general-purpose; the refinement may need either (a) a narrower variant alongside the general one, or (b) a full reshape with the general behaviour deprecated. Decide at M2 design time. Rename to `/shape:outcome-tasks` is the operator's preference (c_a406e2).
2. **Three new skills is a lot for one initiative.** `/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`. Each is justified individually, but it does push against universal Rule B6 ("Generalise on the third occurrence, not the first"). The mitigation: each skill has at least two invocation paths (operator-manual + worker-automated), so the "third occurrence" rule is effectively pre-satisfied. Still worth being explicit about in the design doc.
3. **No formal landing for the sizing decision's *output*.** `/shape:outcome-tasks` decides "split into 3 stacks." That decision needs to land somewhere actionable — worktree file, Linear sub-tickets, or inline implementer prompt? Currently undefined; flag for M2 design doc.

**Counts:**

- Existing skills reused as-is: 9
- Existing skills refined for narrower focus: 1 (`/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` → `/shape:outcome-tasks`)
- New skills authored: 3 (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`)
- New drain-cycle CLI subcommands: 2 (`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify`)
- New non-skill scaffolding (`verify` label parser, run-log fields, OTel attrs, grading template): drain-cycle-internal.

### Open audit decisions (to fold into §10 of the redraft alongside existing open decisions)

A. **`/shape:outcome-tasks` rename adoption** — confirm renaming the existing `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` skill to `/shape:outcome-tasks` for symmetric pairing with `/shape:outcome-verify`. Cost: cross-references in other repos and `agent-skills-shaper` may need updating. Default: adopt the rename.

B. **Refinement style for `/shape:outcome-tasks`** — full reshape of the existing skill (deprecating the general-purpose behaviour), or a narrower variant alongside it? Flag for M2 design doc.

C. **Three-new-skill budget** — confirm comfort with authoring three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`) as part of this initiative, or push some into a sibling skill-authoring initiative.

D. **Where the sizing decision lands** — worktree file? Linear sub-tickets? Inline implementer prompt? Flag for M2 design doc; doesn't need answering now.

# Plan — redraft `docs/initiative-multi-agent-collaboration.md` (round 4 reshape)

## Context

The initiative as currently drafted (rounds 1–3) covers two worker-internal roles: a *planner* (pre-implementation) and a *verifier* (pre-Done). PR-side roles were explicitly out of scope. In conversation today, the operator clarified:

- The "planner" framing was wrong — they already do that planning themselves; what they actually want before implementation is a **Pre-Implementation Reviewer** that decides *sizing/stack-shape* and checks *AC sufficiency*, not one that does the planning.
- The "verifier" overlaps with code-review (which already happens via a sub-agent during implementation). The Outcome Verifier is a *different* check: did the implementer build what the spec said, and were the stated outcomes reached?
- Two more roles belong in this initiative: a **Pre-Human-Review Preparer** that ensures every PR has What / Why / Focus structure (and routes to auto-merge if nothing critical), and a **PR-Feedback Responder** that addresses human review comments on Graphite PRs autonomously.
- One initiative, not two — but with explicit milestones so the bigger scope is tractable.

This plan describes the redraft for `docs/initiative-multi-agent-collaboration.md` that incorporates the reshape, then sends it back through crit (round 4).

## Final roster (the redrafted doc will list this in §1 / §5)

Seven roles total. Role 0 is the human; roles 1–6 are agent-driven. Each role maps 1:1 to exactly one entry in the skill inventory below — the `Skill / CLI` column makes that mapping explicit.

| # | Role | Phase | What it does | Skill / CLI (see inventory) | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | **Operator** (you) | Before & throughout | Defines the *why* (initiative goal/KRs) and the *what* (outcome-focused Linear issue, agent-assisted but human-led) | Operator-side skill table (`/shape:initiative`, `/spec-driven-development`, `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` for ticket assembly, `/crit:crit`, etc.) | — |
| 1 | **Task Shaper** *(was: Pre-Implementation Reviewer)* | Before code | Refines the outcome-focused task into enriched ACs + sizing/stack-shape decision (1 stack vs N) + per-stack implementation task list. Carve-out on routing: even small work goes down human-review path if it requires human review AND won't block queue. | `/shape:outcome-tasks` (refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) | **[R]** |
| 2 | **Implementer** | Middle | Worker session: build/test/source-verify/in-session-code-review/debug/commit chain. The standard "the worker writing code" phase. | 6 existing skills (`/incremental-implementation`, `/test-driven-development`, `/source-driven-development`, `/code-review-and-quality`, `/git-workflow-and-versioning`, `/debugging-and-error-recovery`) | [E] |
| 3 | **Outcome Verifier** | After implementation, before Done | Strictly: did the implementer build what the spec described, and were the outcomes reached? Not code-review (that ran in role 2). | `/shape:outcome-verify` | **[N]** |
| 4 | **PR Preparer** *(was: Pre-Human-Review Preparer)* | After Outcome Verifier passes, before PR submission | Walks each PR (or Graphite stack PR), writes the What / Why / What-reviewer-should-focus-on body. Routes to auto-merge if no critical focus items. | `/shape:pr-prepare` | **[N]** |
| 5 | **PR Responder** *(was: PR-Feedback Responder)* | Post-submission, on new human review comments | Reads new review comments, makes the fix in the right worktree, pushes to the stack. Trigger model has three plausible variants (see open decisions). | `/shape:pr-respond` | **[N]** |
| 6 | **Grader** (post-drain) | After worker session finishes | Drafts the per-ticket grade template at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with `status: draft`. Operator confirms (`draft` → `confirmed`). | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | [—] |

**Mapping check.** Six agent-driven roles (1–6); six entries in the worker-side skill inventory below. 1:1. Role 6 is the one outlier — it's CLI-driven rather than skill-driven (per c_832b0f), so the `Skill / CLI` column shows the CLI subcommand instead of a `/shape:` path.

**Naming alignment.** Roles 1, 4, 5 were renamed to mirror their skill names (`outcome-tasks` → Task Shaper, `pr-prepare` → PR Preparer, `pr-respond` → PR Responder). Roles 2, 3, 6 already aligned.

**Operator still in the loop at three points:** cycle planning (role 0), confirming grade templates (`draft` → `confirmed`), and reviewing PRs that route down the human-review path.

## KR reshape

The override-down-to-2 from round 3 is no longer needed — the broader scope has three real, non-overlapping failure modes that each deserve a KR. Proposed new KR set (final wording in the redraft):

- **KR1 — merge-clean rate on hard tickets** *(stretch, bet)*. Unchanged in spirit. ≥ 3 of next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits.
- **KR2 — zero silent Dones** *(commit, brake)*. Every Done in Linear under the new flow has an Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log. Zero exceptions.
- **KR3 — every PR submitted under the new flow has the What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge** *(commit, brake)*. PR Preparer either produced the structured body, or marked the PR auto-mergeable.

Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) now passes naturally; the round-3 override note in §6.3 is deleted.

The PR Responder doesn't get its own KR in v1 — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback) and the Layer 1/2 dimensions stay balanced (correctness / maintenance / discipline) without a fourth KR.

## Appetite & milestone restructure (this is the big change to §7)

Old §7 was a flat list of 12 slices. New §7 reorganises around five milestones. Each milestone has a stop-the-line gate: if the milestone's gate fails, the initiative pauses for a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The total slice count grows from ~12 to ~18–22.

- **M1 — Recording infrastructure.** `verify` label parser; run-log schema fields (`flow`, `outcome_verdict`, `prep_verdict`, optional `responder_runs[]`); matching OTel attributes on `drain.issue`; `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession. Gate: an end-to-end "verify"-labelled ticket drains, records the new fields, no behaviour change yet.
- **M2 — Worker-internal loop.** Task Shaper (`/shape:outcome-tasks` refined), Implementer integration (no change beyond contract), Outcome Verifier (`/shape:outcome-verify` — new skill; `/code-review-and-quality` is *not* the right fit here), halt-on-fail path. Gate: 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate fail.
- **M3 — PR-shape gate.** PR Preparer (`/shape:pr-prepare`); structured PR body writer; auto-merge routing logic; integration with Graphite stack output. Gate: 3 stacks emitted, all with What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels.
- **M4 — PR-feedback loop.** PR Responder (`/shape:pr-respond`); trigger mechanism (defaulting to polling at drain-start, per Q2 conversation — open decision below); fix-push to stack; idempotency / dedup of already-addressed comments. Gate: one real PR with operator feedback gets addressed by the responder end-to-end.
- **M5 — Grading + cost + close-out.** Per-ticket grade template (with auto-draft from round 2); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify`; silent-Done detection; cost-impact handling in `limits.yml`; post-launch review against KRs. Gate: KR grading runs; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} decided.

The plan file the worker eventually breaks this into (via `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) will name the individual slices per milestone.

## §9 (out of scope) — net change

**Moves into scope:** PR Responder / feedback loop (M4); PR Preparer (M3).

**Stays out of scope:**
- Parallel drains (still orthogonal; decision §8 unchanged).
- Authoring new skills in `agent-skills-shaper` — *except* if M2's open decision below concludes the Outcome Verifier needs a new bespoke skill, in which case that one is allowed.

**New out-of-scope items to add:**
- Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows (single-user product per §3).
- Replacing Graphite itself (the PR-feedback work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow).

## Open decisions to flag in new §10 (round 4)

These are the genuinely open ones the operator hasn't pinned yet. Each will be a numbered point in §10:

1. **Outcome Verifier as a new skill** — confirmed in the audit as `[N]` (`/shape:outcome-verify`); flagged here for §10 visibility because it pairs symmetrically with `/shape:outcome-tasks`. No alternative on the table.
2. **PR Responder trigger model** — three plausible options (polling at drain-start / GitHub Action routing to Linear ticket / one-keystroke `drain-cycle respond <pr>`). Default in the redraft: *polling at drain-start*, per operator's daemon-aversion. Webhook clarification (no new subscription needed; constraint is *where the agent runs*) summarised in §1.
3. **Sizing rule threshold** — per Q3, default to agent-judged + reasoning logged. Confirm there's no fallback numeric threshold (e.g. ≥ N files) wanted as a guardrail.
4. **KR3 commit-vs-stretch** — currently proposed as commit/brake. Confirm or flip to stretch.
5. **Milestone gates as kill points** — confirm gates are stop-the-line in both directions (proceed OR kill), not just "review and continue."
6. **PR Preparer and auto-merge mechanism** — `gh pr merge --auto` (GitHub native) vs Graphite's merge queue (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-style). Default to Graphite-native since the rest of the PR workflow is Graphite.

## Doc sections — what changes, what stays

| Section | Change |
|---|---|
| §1 Raw idea | **Rewrite.** Lead with the six-role roster; webhook clarification summary; one-initiative scope statement. |
| §2 Problem-or-solution gate | **Minor edit.** Still passes; update language so "input refinement" reads as "Pre-Implementation Reviewer" and add the PR-side failure mode. |
| §3 Project type | **No change.** Still Type 2. |
| §4 Negative outcome | **Extend.** Add the PR-side failure modes: PRs land without focus guidance, human review comments go unaddressed until operator manually re-invokes. |
| §5 Affected repos | **Minor edit.** Note that `agent-skills-shaper` may receive one new skill (Outcome Verifier) if M2's open decision concludes that route. Otherwise drain-cycle remains the only repo touched. |
| §6 Goal + KRs | **Rewrite.** New Goal sentence covering both worker-internal and PR-side surfaces. Three KRs (KR1 ship-clean, KR2 zero silent Dones, KR3 PR body structure or auto-merge). Drop the round-3 override on rule 4. |
| §7 Appetite | **Rewrite.** Move from flat 12-slice list to five-milestone structure (M1–M5) with gates. Total grows to ~18–22 issues. |
| §8 Kill condition | **Edit.** Add per-milestone gate behaviour. Keep the existing 5-tickets-across-2-cycles whole-initiative kill as the M2 gate; extend with M3/M4-specific kill cues. |
| §9 Out of scope | **Rewrite.** Per § "§9 net change" above. |
| §10 Open review points | **Reset.** Round 1/2/3 marked resolved; new round-4 list per "open decisions" above. |
| §11 Next steps | **No change.** |

Pairs symmetrically with `/shape:outcome-tasks` (row 1) — one defines the outcome-shaped task list, the other verifies the outcome was delivered. |
| **4. Pre-Human-Review Preparer** — PR body + auto-merge route | new skill — working name `/shape:pr-prepare` | **[N]** | Reads stack PRs from Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body; tags auto-merge eligible vs human-review-needed. |
| **5. PR-Feedback Responder** — address review comments | new skill — working name `/shape:pr-respond` | **[N]** | Reads PR review threads via Graphite MCP, makes the fix in the right worktree, pushes to stack. Trigger is drain-cycle-side (polling / Action / one-keystroke), but the skill itself is the same primitive regardless of trigger. |
| **6. Grader** (auto-draft) | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | **[—]** | Per operator review (c_832b0f): this is a CLI command on drain-cycle, not a skill. One-shot internal prompt: ticket + run-log + diff → fills the grade template with `status: draft`. Operator can invoke it manually on past tickets to retro-fill grades. |

### Non-skill primitives the worker still uses

Included so the audit is complete — these aren't skills but are the rest of the composition surface.

| Primitive | What it is | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__*` | Linear MCP — read/write issues, comments, status, labels, cycles | The work-queue interface. |
| `mcp__graphite__*` | Graphite MCP — manage stacked PRs | The PR-side interface. |
| `mcp__honeycomb__*` | Honeycomb MCP — observability | Operability surface; OTel spans already ship since `3eae2f5`. |
| `drain-cycle` CLI | Dispatcher: worktree creation, worker spawning, run-log writing, grader invocation | Orchestration, not a skill. |
| `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` | New subcommand — drafts the per-ticket grade template after a drain finishes (or retro on a past issue) | Per c_832b0f, replaces what was previously sketched as `/shape:grade-draft`. |
| `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` | KR-grading subcommand — reads confirmed grade templates, reports pass-rate, warns about un-confirmed drafts | Carried over from round-3 doc. |
| `gh` / `gt` CLIs | GitHub + Graphite shells | Called from inside skills (e.g. `/git-workflow-and-versioning`). |

### UNIX-philosophy audit — findings

**Strengths:**

1. **Shared toolkit confirmed.** Every skill in the worker table is also invokable by you manually — decision 10's symlink working as intended. The four `[N]` skills inherit this property because they'll be authored as standard skills, not drain-cycle-internal logic.
2. **Each skill is single-purpose.** No skill in either table does two unrelated things; the role-to-skill mapping is roughly 1:1, and where it's many-to-one (e.g. Implementer using five skills sequentially) it's because the *role* spans multiple phases, not because a skill is overloaded.
3. **Reuse over new authoring where defensible.** Five of the six worker roles use existing skills; only three slots need genuinely new skills (plus one borderline).

**Warnings:**

1. **`/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` refinement scope.** Refining the existing skill to focus on a single outcome-focused task is conceptually clean (c_b76ab7), but the existing skill is general-purpose; the refinement may need either (a) a narrower variant alongside the general one, or (b) a full reshape with the general behaviour deprecated. Decide at M2 design time. Rename to `/shape:outcome-tasks` is the operator's preference (c_a406e2).
2. **Three new skills is a lot for one initiative.** `/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`. Each is justified individually, but it does push against universal Rule B6 ("Generalise on the third occurrence, not the first"). The mitigation: each skill has at least two invocation paths (operator-manual + worker-automated), so the "third occurrence" rule is effectively pre-satisfied. Still worth being explicit about in the design doc.
3. **No formal landing for the sizing decision's *output*.** `/shape:outcome-tasks` decides "split into 3 stacks." That decision needs to land somewhere actionable — worktree file, Linear sub-tickets, or inline implementer prompt? Currently undefined; flag for M2 design doc.

**Counts:**

- Existing skills reused as-is: 9
- Existing skills refined for narrower focus: 1 (`/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` → `/shape:outcome-tasks`)
- New skills authored: 3 (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`)
- New drain-cycle CLI subcommands: 2 (`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify`)
- New non-skill scaffolding (`verify` label parser, run-log fields, OTel attrs, grading template): drain-cycle-internal.

### Open audit decisions (to fold into §10 of the redraft alongside existing open decisions)

A. **`/shape:outcome-tasks` rename adoption** — confirm renaming the existing `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` skill to `/shape:outcome-tasks` for symmetric pairing with `/shape:outcome-verify`. Cost: cross-references in other repos and `agent-skills-shaper` may need updating. Default: adopt the rename.

B. **Refinement style for `/shape:outcome-tasks`** — full reshape of the existing skill (deprecating the general-purpose behaviour), or a narrower variant alongside it? Flag for M2 design doc.

C. **Three-new-skill budget** — confirm comfort with authoring three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`) as part of this initiative, or push some into a sibling skill-authoring initiative.

D. **Where the sizing decision lands** — worktree file? Linear sub-tickets? Inline implementer prompt? Flag for M2 design doc; doesn't need answering now.

content.text: Plan approved — now executing the redraft. Writing the new `docs/initiative-multi-agent-collaboration.md`, then re-launching crit for round 4.

# Draft Linear initiative — multi-agent collaboration for correctness

**Status:** draft for review (round 4 reshape; Step 5 of `initiative-shape`). Linear project NOT created yet — pending operator confirmation (Step 6) and the 11-rule verification rubric (Step 6.5).

**How to use this doc:** leave inline feedback on any section. The roster in §1, the KRs in §6, and the milestone structure in §7 are the primary review surfaces.

---

## §1 Raw idea (Step 1)

drain-cycle evolves from "drain a Linear cycle unattended" toward a **UNIX-philosophy dispatcher for composable skill primitives** — Linear tickets as the work queue, skills+MCPs as the small tools, and `drain-cycle` itself as the batch-mode shell that composes them. Operator (you) drives the *why* (initiative goal + KRs) and the *what* (outcome-focused Linear issue); agents handle the *how* through a defined set of skills. The same skills are invokable manually at the keyboard OR automatically inside a worker session — design decision §10's symlink already makes this true.

The operator failure mode this initiative addresses: **complex tickets that can't be sliced further** where the worker forgets details, under-verifies, or submits an unstructured PR — followed by human review comments that pile up while the operator manually re-invokes the agent to address each round.

The bet: six agent-driven roles end-to-end, each backed by a skill (or CLI subcommand) that fits the UNIX-philosophy frame:

| # | Role | Phase | Skill / CLI | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | **Operator** (you) | Throughout | Operator-side skill table; defines *why* + *what* | — |
| 1 | **Task Shaper** | Before code | `/shape:outcome-tasks` *(refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`)* | [R] |
| 2 | **Implementer** | Middle | 6 existing skills (incremental-implementation, test-driven-development, source-driven-development, code-review-and-quality, git-workflow-and-versioning, debugging-and-error-recovery) | [E] |
| 3 | **Outcome Verifier** | After implementation, before Done | `/shape:outcome-verify` *(new)* | [N] |
| 4 | **PR Preparer** | After Outcome Verifier passes, before PR submission | `/shape:pr-prepare` *(new)* | [N] |
| 5 | **PR Responder** | On new human review comments | `/shape:pr-respond` *(new)* | [N] |
| 6 | **Grader** (post-drain) | After worker session finishes | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | [—] |

Roles 1 and 3 are a **complementary pair**: `/shape:outcome-tasks` defines the outcome-shaped task list at the start; `/shape:outcome-verify` validates the outcome at the end. Roles 4 and 5 are the **PR-side pair**: Preparer shapes outbound PRs, Responder addresses inbound review feedback. Role 6 is operator-confirm scaffolding for grading the initiative against its KRs.

**Constraints carried from prior rounds:**
- Subscription auth only (Claude Code / Codex CLI subprocess) — no API-key models. GitHub webhooks themselves are free, but the agent itself can't run inside GitHub Actions; agent work has to happen on the operator's machine.
- Correctness > throughput. No parallel drains. No throughput-focused changes.

## §2 Problem-or-solution gate (Step 2)

**Passes.** The operator-impacting failures are named and specific:

1. **Silent Done on hard tickets.** Complex unsliceable tickets get drained; worker self-asserts Done; produced diff misses AC; operator finds out at PR review and fixes by hand.
2. **Unstructured PR submission.** PRs land without What/Why/Focus guidance; reviewer has to guess what to look at, or the operator manually rewrites the body.
3. **Stalled feedback loops.** Human review comments accumulate on stacked PRs; operator manually re-invokes claude to address each round.

The six-role roster is the proposed solution; each role maps to one of the failure modes (Task Shaper + Outcome Verifier address #1; PR Preparer addresses #2; PR Responder addresses #3).

## §3 Project type (Step 2.5)

**Type 2 — personal product, single-user.** Confirmed.

Rationale: drain-cycle is described in `AGENTS.md` as "Python, single-user, personal product"; the artefact being evolved is the product itself; any new skills/MCPs are primitives drain-cycle invokes, not the headline deliverable; one user (you), so growth/MAU KRs are categorically inapplicable per Type 2 playbook.

## §4 Negative outcome if not solved

If the initiative is not built:

- **Hard tickets continue to ship Done with missing AC.** The worker self-asserts Done (per design decision §1); the only feedback is at PR-review time, when the operator either accepts the gap or pushes manual fix commits. The unattended-drain promise breaks on exactly the tickets where it matters most.
- **PR review burden falls entirely on the human.** Reviewers (operator + collaborators) open PRs with no guidance on what to focus on; auto-mergeable changes get the same scrutiny as risky ones; review throughput is slower than it needs to be.
- **Human review feedback creates a manual re-invocation tax.** Each round of comments on a stacked PR requires the operator to context-switch back into the worktree, re-launch claude, and shepherd the agent through the fix. The agent ecosystem stops at code-submission time instead of running end-to-end through merge.

## §5 Affected repos

- **`drain-cycle`** — primary. Worker integration of roles 1–5, run-log schema extensions, `verify` label mechanism, CLI subcommands (`grade-draft`, `grade --flow=verify`), supersession of design decision §1, OTel attribute additions.
- **`agent-skills-shaper`** — secondary, conditional. Receives one refined skill (`/shape:outcome-tasks`, refined from `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) and three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`). Open decision C in §10 controls whether new-skill authoring happens inside this initiative or splits to a sibling.

## §6 Goal and Key results

The KRs are written in plain English — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each in 30 seconds. Rubric annotations (Layer 1 / Layer 2 dimensions) live in italic audit-footers under each KR.

### §6.0 Goal

**For** the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear tickets, **we want** six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done, structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review comments — **so that** complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC, PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't create a manual re-invocation tax.

### §6.1 KR1 (stretch) — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow, ≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*

- **plain-English version:** when I run drain-cycle on a hard ticket I couldn't break into smaller pieces, six agent roles take it from intake to merged PR. The bet is that this catches enough real problems and structures the PRs well enough that most merge cleanly without me having to push fix commits.
- **baseline:** 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today.
- **target:** ≥ 3 of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits.
- **measured over:** the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow (~2 cycles at current cadence).
- **how we'll grade it:** per-ticket grade markdown at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms `draft` → `confirmed`); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate across the window.

*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*

### §6.2 KR2 (commit) — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows the Outcome Verifier ran and produced a verdict. Protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log.
- **measured over:** every cycle that runs the new flow, for the initiative's duration (cap at 10 cycles if the initiative runs long).
- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.

*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*

### §6.3 KR3 (commit) — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the PR Preparer either writes a structured What / Why / Focus body and routes the PR for human review, or it correctly identifies the PR as auto-mergeable (no critical focus items) and routes it to auto-merge. Either way, every PR comes out with a documented routing decision and a reviewable body.
- **baseline:** today, PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance.
- **target:** 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning attached.
- **measured over:** every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration.
- **how we'll grade it:** PR Preparer writes its decision to a `prep_verdict` field on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line.

*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*

### §6.4 KR rule check

Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) passes: three KRs covering three distinct failure modes (correctness, maintenance discipline, PR-shape discipline). Rule 5b (distinct dimensions) passes: correctness / maintenance / discipline. No override needed.

The PR Responder (role 5) doesn't get its own KR — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback). Adding a fourth KR for responder-specific metrics would push toward padding given KR1 already exercises that surface end-to-end.

## §7 Appetite

**~18–22 issues across five milestones.** Each milestone has a stop-the-line gate; if the gate fails, the initiative pauses for a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The plan file the worker eventually breaks this into (via `/shape:outcome-tasks` or its predecessor) will name individual slices per milestone.

### M1 — Recording infrastructure

Build the data layer first, behaviour later.

- `verify` Linear label parser added alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution in `labels.py`.
- Run-log schema fields: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow), `outcome_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null`), `prep_verdict` (object: `{result, route, reasoning}` or `null`), `responder_runs[]` (array of objects: `{comment_ids[], invoked_at, result}` — appended each time the responder runs).
- Matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span.
- `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession: write the successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded.

**Gate:** an end-to-end `verify`-labelled ticket drains; new fields populate (all as `null` since no roles are wired yet); existing behaviour unchanged.

### M2 — Worker-internal loop (Task Shaper, Outcome Verifier, halt path)

- **Task Shaper** (`/shape:outcome-tasks`): refined skill that takes a Linear issue and produces enriched AC checklist + sizing decision (single stack vs N stacks) + per-stack task list. Open decision B in §10 governs whether this is a full reshape of `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` or a narrower variant alongside.
- **Implementer integration**: worker session pulls the Task Shaper output as context; no other implementer changes.
- **Outcome Verifier** (`/shape:outcome-verify`): new skill. Inputs: ticket body + Task Shaper output + diff + run-log. Output: structured verdict (pass / fail + findings) written to `outcome_verdict`.
- **Halt-on-fail path**: extends decision §8 / §9 machinery — on Outcome Verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.

**Gate:** 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate failure (e.g. a synthetic ticket whose AC the implementer can't satisfy).

### M3 — PR-shape gate (PR Preparer)

- **PR Preparer** (`/shape:pr-prepare`): new skill. Reads stack PRs via Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body on each; decides auto-merge vs human-review per the carve-out rule (small + no human review needed → auto-merge; otherwise → human-review).
- **Auto-merge mechanism**: defaults to Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-equivalent). Open decision F in §10 covers the alternative (`gh pr merge --auto`).
- Records decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry.

**Gate:** 3 stacks emitted, all with structured What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels. PR-body assertion in `drain-cycle grade` passes on all three.

### M4 — PR-feedback loop (PR Responder)

- **PR Responder** (`/shape:pr-respond`): new skill. Reads new review comments on stack PRs via Graphite MCP; makes the fix in the right worktree; pushes to the stack; appends to `responder_runs[]`.
- **Trigger model**: defaults to polling-at-drain-start (each `drain-cycle drain` invocation checks for un-addressed review comments before picking up new tickets, and addresses any found). Open decision D in §10 covers the alternatives (GitHub Action → Linear ticket; one-keystroke `drain-cycle respond <pr>`).
- **Idempotency**: each comment ID tracked in `responder_runs[].comment_ids[]` so the same comment isn't addressed twice across drains.

**Gate:** one real PR with operator-left review feedback gets addressed end-to-end by the responder; fix lands on the stack; run-log shows the responder run.

### M5 — Grading + cost + close-out

- **Per-ticket grade template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with checklist + `status:` field.
- **`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand**: one-shot grading prompt. Invoked automatically on drain completion (writes `status: draft`); also invokable manually by the operator for retro-fill on past tickets.
- **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand**: reads `confirmed` grade files, reports pass-rate across a ticket window, warns about un-confirmed drafts.
- **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): exit-code-nonzero on any `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict == null` entry.
- **Cost-impact handling**: measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
- **Post-launch review**: write a Linear comment on each KR's status at end of the initiative window; either ship cleanly, pause, or kill.

**Gate:** KR grading runs end-to-end; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} is decided and recorded.

## §8 Kill condition

Two layers:

**Per-milestone gates as stop-the-line points.** Each M1–M5 gate is proceed-or-kill, not "review and continue." Failure at a gate triggers a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The clearest kill at each:

- M1 — schema additions cause unanticipated breakage in existing run-log readers or downstream tooling.
- M2 — 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles produce zero true-positive findings from Outcome Verifier (every halt was a false positive). Carried from the round-3 doc.
- M3 — PR Preparer's bodies don't measurably reduce reviewer cognitive load (signal: operator subjectively prefers the raw worker-produced PRs).
- M4 — PR Responder either (a) can't address comments cleanly without operator intervention >50% of the time, or (b) the trigger model produces dropped/missed comments.

**Action on kill (any milestone):** revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields — they stay in run log + OTel because they're cheap and useful for future investigation; record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.

## §9 Out of scope (explicitly)

**Moves into scope (vs round-3):** PR Preparer (M3); PR Responder / feedback loop (M4).

**Stays out of scope:**
- **Parallel drains.** Decision §8's serial-drain stance unchanged.
- **Replacing Graphite.** The PR-side work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow; switching PR-management tools is a separate decision.
- **Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows.** Single-user product per §3.

## §10 Open review points (round 4)

**Resolved in rounds 1–3:**
- ~~§6 KRs need plain English~~ [r1, c_7918fa]
- ~~§6.1 clarify what's new (metrics/logs?)~~ [r1, c_b1b472]
- ~~§6.1 single-trial too weak; need template for multi-ticket grading~~ [r1, c_d29df7]
- ~~§6.3 KR3 redundant with KR1; offered override~~ [r1, c_a2a5ee] *(re-introduced as PR-structure KR in round 4 with new scope)*
- ~~§7 slice 3 `flow:` label premature abstraction~~ [r1, c_dacf16]
- ~~§7 slice 1 — which skill for the planner spike?~~ [r2, c_befa07] *(planner spike dropped entirely in round 4)*
- ~~§7 slice 10 — grading template friction~~ [r2, c_1d55a4]
- ~~Roster names → skill names alignment~~ [plan-r2, c_7e2295]
- ~~Pre-Implementation Reviewer skill identity~~ [plan-r1, c_b76ab7] → resolved as `/shape:outcome-tasks`
- ~~Outcome Verifier complementary naming~~ [plan-r1, c_a406e2] → `/shape:outcome-verify`
- ~~Grader as skill vs CLI~~ [plan-r1, c_832b0f] → CLI, not skill

**Open for round 4:**

A. **`/shape:outcome-tasks` rename adoption** — confirm renaming the existing `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` skill to `/shape:outcome-tasks` for symmetric pairing with `/shape:outcome-verify`. Cost: cross-references in other repos and `agent-skills-shaper` may need updating. Default: adopt.

B. **Refinement style for `/shape:outcome-tasks`** — full reshape of the existing skill (deprecating the general-purpose behaviour), or a narrower variant alongside? Flag for M2 design doc; doesn't need answering now.

C. **Three-new-skill budget inside this initiative** — confirm comfort authoring `/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond` as part of this initiative, or push some into a sibling skill-authoring initiative.

D. **PR Responder trigger model** — three plausible options: polling at drain-start (default; no daemon) / GitHub Action routing to Linear ticket (no operator-side daemon, but Action setup) / one-keystroke `drain-cycle respond <pr>` (most explicit, most manual). Default: polling.

E. **Sizing rule for Task Shaper** — agent-judged + reasoning logged (default), or fallback numeric threshold (e.g. ≥ N files → split) as a guardrail?

F. **Auto-merge mechanism** — Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`) vs GitHub-native (`gh pr merge --auto`). Default: Graphite-native.

G. **KR3 commit-vs-stretch** — currently proposed as commit/brake. Confirm or flip to stretch.

H. **Where the sizing decision lands** — worktree file? Linear sub-tickets? Inline implementer prompt? Flag for M2 design doc; doesn't need answering now.

I. **Milestone gates as kill points** — confirm gates are stop-the-line in both directions (proceed OR kill), not just "review and continue."

## §11 Next steps

1. You leave inline feedback on this doc.
2. I revise affected sections.
3. Repeat until you confirm (Step 6 gate).
4. I run the 11-rule verification rubric (Step 6.5) and report any failures. Rule 4 should pass on its own (3 KRs); no override needed.
5. Once all 11 rules pass, I create the Linear project via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_project` with the synthesised body, status Planned.
6. Sibling work (M1 design doc) becomes the next phase.

| # | Role | Phase | Skill / CLI | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|

| 1 | **Task Shaper** | Before code | `/shape:outcome-tasks` *(refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`)* | [R] |
| 2 | **Implementer** | Middle | 6 existing skills (incremental-implementation, test-driven-development, source-driven-development, code-review-and-quality, git-workflow-and-versioning, debugging-and-error-recovery) | [E] |
| 3 | **Outcome Verifier** | After implementation, before Done | `/shape:outcome-verify` *(new)* | [N] |
| 4 | **PR Preparer** | After Outcome Verifier passes, before PR submission | `/shape:pr-prepare` *(new)* | [N] |
| 5 | **PR Responder** | On new human review comments | `/shape:pr-respond` *(new)* | [N] |
| 6 | **Grader** (post-drain) | After worker session finishes | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | [—] |

Roles 1 and 3 are a **complementary pair**: `/shape:outcome-tasks` defines the outcome-shaped task list at the start; `/shape:outcome-verify` validates the outcome at the end. Roles 4 and 5 are the **PR-side pair**: Preparer shapes outbound PRs, Responder addresses inbound review feedback. Role 6 is operator-confirm scaffolding for grading the initiative against its KRs.

**Constraints carried from prior rounds:**
- Subscription auth only (Claude Code / Codex CLI subprocess) — no API-key models. GitHub webhooks themselves are free, but the agent itself can't run inside GitHub Actions; agent work has to happen on the operator's machine.
- Correctness > throughput. No parallel drains. No throughput-focused changes.

## §2 Problem-or-solution gate (Step 2)

**Passes.** The operator-impacting failures are named and specific:

1. **Silent Done on hard tickets.** Complex unsliceable tickets get drained; worker self-asserts Done; produced diff misses AC; operator finds out at PR review and fixes by hand.
2. **Unstructured PR submission.** PRs land without What/Why/Focus guidance; reviewer has to guess what to look at, or the operator manually rewrites the body.
3. **Stalled feedback loops.** Human review comments accumulate on stacked PRs; operator manually re-invokes claude to address each round.

The six-role roster is the proposed solution; each role maps to one of the failure modes (Task Shaper + Outcome Verifier address #1; PR Preparer addresses #2; PR Responder addresses #3).

## §3 Project type (Step 2.5)

**Type 2 — personal product, single-user.** Confirmed.

Rationale: drain-cycle is described in `AGENTS.md` as "Python, single-user, personal product"; the artefact being evolved is the product itself; any new skills/MCPs are primitives drain-cycle invokes, not the headline deliverable; one user (you), so growth/MAU KRs are categorically inapplicable per Type 2 playbook.

## §4 Negative outcome if not solved

If the initiative is not built:

- **Hard tickets continue to ship Done with missing AC.** The worker self-asserts Done (per design decision §1); the only feedback is at PR-review time, when the operator either accepts the gap or pushes manual fix commits. The unattended-drain promise breaks on exactly the tickets where it matters most.
- **PR review burden falls entirely on the human.** Reviewers (operator + collaborators) open PRs with no guidance on what to focus on; auto-mergeable changes get the same scrutiny as risky ones; review throughput is slower than it needs to be.
- **Human review feedback creates a manual re-invocation tax.** Each round of comments on a stacked PR requires the operator to context-switch back into the worktree, re-launch claude, and shepherd the agent through the fix. The agent ecosystem stops at code-submission time instead of running end-to-end through merge.

## §5 Affected repos

- **`drain-cycle`** — primary. Worker integration of roles 1–5, run-log schema extensions, `verify` label mechanism, CLI subcommands (`grade-draft`, `grade --flow=verify`), supersession of design decision §1, OTel attribute additions.
- **`agent-skills-shaper`** — secondary, conditional. Receives one refined skill (`/shape:outcome-tasks`, refined from `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) and three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`). Open decision C in §10 controls whether new-skill authoring happens inside this initiative or splits to a sibling.

## §6 Goal and Key results

The KRs are written in plain English — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each in 30 seconds. Rubric annotations (Layer 1 / Layer 2 dimensions) live in italic audit-footers under each KR.

### §6.0 Goal

**For** the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear tickets, **we want** six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done, structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review comments — **so that** complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC, PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't create a manual re-invocation tax.

### §6.1 KR1 (stretch) — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow, ≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*

- **plain-English version:** when I run drain-cycle on a hard ticket I couldn't break into smaller pieces, six agent roles take it from intake to merged PR. The bet is that this catches enough real problems and structures the PRs well enough that most merge cleanly without me having to push fix commits.
- **baseline:** 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today.
- **target:** ≥ 3 of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits.
- **measured over:** the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow (~2 cycles at current cadence).
- **how we'll grade it:** per-ticket grade markdown at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms `draft` → `confirmed`); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate across the window.

*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*

### §6.2 KR2 (commit) — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows the Outcome Verifier ran and produced a verdict. Protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log.
- **measured over:** every cycle that runs the new flow, for the initiative's duration (cap at 10 cycles if the initiative runs long).
- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.

*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*

### §6.3 KR3 (commit) — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the PR Preparer either writes a structured What / Why / Focus body and routes the PR for human review, or it correctly identifies the PR as auto-mergeable (no critical focus items) and routes it to auto-merge. Either way, every PR comes out with a documented routing decision and a reviewable body.
- **baseline:** today, PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance.
- **target:** 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning attached.
- **measured over:** every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration.
- **how we'll grade it:** PR Preparer writes its decision to a `prep_verdict` field on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line.

*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*

### §6.4 KR rule check

Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) passes: three KRs covering three distinct failure modes (correctness, maintenance discipline, PR-shape discipline). Rule 5b (distinct dimensions) passes: correctness / maintenance / discipline. No override needed.

The PR Responder (role 5) doesn't get its own KR — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback). Adding a fourth KR for responder-specific metrics would push toward padding given KR1 already exercises that surface end-to-end.

## §7 Appetite

**~18–22 issues across five milestones.** Each milestone has a stop-the-line gate; if the gate fails, the initiative pauses for a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The plan file the worker eventually breaks this into (via `/shape:outcome-tasks` or its predecessor) will name individual slices per milestone.

### M1 — Recording infrastructure

Build the data layer first, behaviour later.

- `verify` Linear label parser added alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution in `labels.py`.
- Run-log schema fields: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow), `outcome_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null`), `prep_verdict` (object: `{result, route, reasoning}` or `null`), `responder_runs[]` (array of objects: `{comment_ids[], invoked_at, result}` — appended each time the responder runs).
- Matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span.
- `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession: write the successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded.

**Gate:** an end-to-end `verify`-labelled ticket drains; new fields populate (all as `null` since no roles are wired yet); existing behaviour unchanged.

### M2 — Worker-internal loop (Task Shaper, Outcome Verifier, halt path)

- **Task Shaper** (`/shape:outcome-tasks`): refined skill that takes a Linear issue and produces enriched AC checklist + sizing decision (single stack vs N stacks) + per-stack task list. Open decision B in §10 governs whether this is a full reshape of `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` or a narrower variant alongside.
- **Implementer integration**: worker session pulls the Task Shaper output as context; no other implementer changes.
- **Outcome Verifier** (`/shape:outcome-verify`): new skill. Inputs: ticket body + Task Shaper output + diff + run-log. Output: structured verdict (pass / fail + findings) written to `outcome_verdict`.
- **Halt-on-fail path**: extends decision §8 / §9 machinery — on Outcome Verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.

**Gate:** 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate failure (e.g. a synthetic ticket whose AC the implementer can't satisfy).

### M3 — PR-shape gate (PR Preparer)

- **PR Preparer** (`/shape:pr-prepare`): new skill. Reads stack PRs via Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body on each; decides auto-merge vs human-review per the carve-out rule (small + no human review needed → auto-merge; otherwise → human-review).
- **Auto-merge mechanism**: defaults to Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-equivalent). Open decision F in §10 covers the alternative (`gh pr merge --auto`).
- Records decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry.

**Gate:** 3 stacks emitted, all with structured What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels. PR-body assertion in `drain-cycle grade` passes on all three.

### M4 — PR-feedback loop (PR Responder)

- **PR Responder** (`/shape:pr-respond`): new skill. Reads new review comments on stack PRs via Graphite MCP; makes the fix in the right worktree; pushes to the stack; appends to `responder_runs[]`.
- **Trigger model**: defaults to polling-at-drain-start (each `drain-cycle drain` invocation checks for un-addressed review comments before picking up new tickets, and addresses any found). Open decision D in §10 covers the alternatives (GitHub Action → Linear ticket; one-keystroke `drain-cycle respond <pr>`).
- **Idempotency**: each comment ID tracked in `responder_runs[].comment_ids[]` so the same comment isn't addressed twice across drains.

**Gate:** one real PR with operator-left review feedback gets addressed end-to-end by the responder; fix lands on the stack; run-log shows the responder run.

### M5 — Grading + cost + close-out

- **Per-ticket grade template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with checklist + `status:` field.
- **`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand**: one-shot grading prompt. Invoked automatically on drain completion (writes `status: draft`); also invokable manually by the operator for retro-fill on past tickets.
- **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand**: reads `confirmed` grade files, reports pass-rate across a ticket window, warns about un-confirmed drafts.
- **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): exit-code-nonzero on any `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict == null` entry.
- **Cost-impact handling**: measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
- **Post-launch review**: write a Linear comment on each KR's status at end of the initiative window; either ship cleanly, pause, or kill.

**Gate:** KR grading runs end-to-end; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} is decided and recorded.

## §8 Kill condition

Two layers:

**Per-milestone gates as stop-the-line points.** Each M1–M5 gate is proceed-or-kill, not "review and continue." Failure at a gate triggers a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The clearest kill at each:

- M1 — schema additions cause unanticipated breakage in existing run-log readers or downstream tooling.
- M2 — 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles produce zero true-positive findings from Outcome Verifier (every halt was a false positive). Carried from the round-3 doc.
- M3 — PR Preparer's bodies don't measurably reduce reviewer cognitive load (signal: operator subjectively prefers the raw worker-produced PRs).
- M4 — PR Responder either (a) can't address comments cleanly without operator intervention >50% of the time, or (b) the trigger model produces dropped/missed comments.

**Action on kill (any milestone):** revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields — they stay in run log + OTel because they're cheap and useful for future investigation; record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.

## §9 Out of scope (explicitly)

**Moves into scope (vs round-3):** PR Preparer (M3); PR Responder / feedback loop (M4).

**Stays out of scope:**
- **Parallel drains.** Decision §8's serial-drain stance unchanged.
- **Replacing Graphite.** The PR-side work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow; switching PR-management tools is a separate decision.
- **Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows.** Single-user product per §3.

-- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows a second-agent review ran and produced a verdict. This protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
-- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per decision §1's contract); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
-- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without a `verifier_verdict` recorded in the run log.

+- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows the Outcome Verifier ran and produced a verdict. Protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
+- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
+- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log.
- **measured over:** every cycle that runs the new flow, for the initiative's duration (cap at 10 cycles if the initiative runs long).

-- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `verifier_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.

+- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.
*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*
-### §6.3 KR count — manual override taken
+### §6.3 KR3 (commit) — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*

-**KR3 dropped per review** (c_a2a5ee). The instrumentation work it would have measured (per-agent step records in the run log and OTel trace) is folded into KR1's "how we'll grade it" as a prerequisite, and remains explicitly scoped as slice 4 of §7. Keeping it as a separate KR was padding given how tightly coupled it is to KR1's grading mechanism — you can't grade KR1 without the instrumentation, so KR1's how-we'll-know already mandates it.

+- **plain-English version:** the PR Preparer either writes a structured What / Why / Focus body and routes the PR for human review, or it correctly identifies the PR as auto-mergeable (no critical focus items) and routes it to auto-merge. Either way, every PR comes out with a documented routing decision and a reviewable body.
+- **baseline:** today, PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance.
+- **target:** 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning attached.
+- **measured over:** every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration.

+- **how we'll grade it:** PR Preparer writes its decision to a `prep_verdict` field on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line.

-This takes the operator's offered manual override of rule 4 (default ≥ 3 KRs). The 11-rule rubric at Step 6.5 will flag rule 4 as failed; the override note in this section is the documented justification.
+*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*
-*Dimensions: correctness / maintenance — distinct per rule 5b. KR count = 2 (manual override per review c_a2a5ee — instrumentation work survives as slice 4 of §7, not as a separate KR).*
+### §6.4 KR rule check

+Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) passes: three KRs covering three distinct failure modes (correctness, maintenance discipline, PR-shape discipline). Rule 5b (distinct dimensions) passes: correctness / maintenance / discipline. No override needed.

+The PR Responder (role 5) doesn't get its own KR — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback). Adding a fourth KR for responder-specific metrics would push toward padding given KR1 already exercises that surface end-to-end.
## §7 Appetite
-**~12 issues — Medium-to-Large.**

+**~18–22 issues across five milestones.** Each milestone has a stop-the-line gate; if the gate fails, the initiative pauses for a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The plan file the worker eventually breaks this into (via `/shape:outcome-tasks` or its predecessor) will name individual slices per milestone.
-Indicative slice sketch (planning-and-task-breakdown produces the real list later; not a commitment to this exact decomposition):
+### M1 — Recording infrastructure

-1. **Planner spike** — operator-driven manual investigation, not a drained ticket. Run `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` interactively on 3 real tickets from a recent cycle (one well-formed, one ambiguous, one you'd call "unsliceable"). Output: a written recommendation in `docs/planner-spike-findings.md` answering — did the skill produce useful AC checklists on all three? did it surface ambiguities the ticket missed? did the "unsliceable" one actually yield a checklist (i.e. was it slicing-resistant, or just under-decomposed)? Decision at the end: proceed / reshape / kill before slice 2. No code changes in this slice; if the spike finds `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` isn't the right skill for the planner role, slice 2's design doc names an alternative (existing skill, new skill, or bespoke prompt).

-2. **shape:design-doc** — architecture for the required-skill-sequence contract: label mechanism, prompt structure, halt behaviour, run-log schema, OTel attribute shape, decision §1 supersession path.

-3. **`verify` opt-in label** — a single flat Linear label that opts a ticket into the new flow. Default (no label) is unchanged; ops/KTLO and simple bugfix tickets continue to drain as today. Generalised to a `flow:` label group only if/when a second flow is introduced — premature for v1 (per review c_dacf16). Parser added to `labels.py` alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution; no group machinery yet.

-4. **Run-log schema + OTel attribute extension** *(prerequisite for KR1; absorbs former KR3 instrumentation)* — add two new fields per run-log entry: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow) and `verifier_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null` if the flow didn't run a verifier). Add matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span. Schema is additive — existing run logs and `grade` continue to work unchanged.

-5–6. **Worker integration: planner pre-step** — when `verify` label is set, invoke `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` as the first action in the worker session; capture the AC checklist into the run log; halt if the planner can't produce one (covered by slice 9's halt machinery).

-7–8. **Worker integration: verifier post-step** — when `verify` label is set, invoke `/code-review-and-quality` against working-tree changes before Done transition; record the verdict in `verifier_verdict`; gate the Linear Done transition on a pass.
-9. **Halt-on-verifier-fail + revert path** — extends decision §8 / §9 machinery: on verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.
-10. **Grading workflow** — designed for low-friction operator confirmation rather than blank-page authorship (per round-2 review c_1d55a4):
-    1. **Per-ticket grade markdown template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` — checklist matching KR1's questions plus a `status:` field (`draft` / `confirmed`).

-    2. **Auto-draft on completion** — when a contract-enabled drain finishes, drain-cycle invokes a one-shot grading agent (single `claude -p` call) with the ticket body + run-log entry + diff, and writes a `status: draft` file at the template path with the agent's first-pass answers. Operator opens the file, reviews, edits where the agent got it wrong, flips `status: confirmed`. Less friction than authoring blank; still operator-owned at the moment of judgement.

-    3. **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand** — reads grade files with `status: confirmed`, reports pass-count and pass-rate across a ticket window; warns about un-confirmed drafts so they don't get forgotten.
-    4. **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): fail any run-log entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `verifier_verdict` is absent.
-11. **Cost-impact handling** — measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
-12. **`docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession** — write a successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded with a link to the new entry.
+Build the data layer first, behaviour later.
+- `verify` Linear label parser added alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution in `labels.py`.

+- Run-log schema fields: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow), `outcome_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null`), `prep_verdict` (object: `{result, route, reasoning}` or `null`), `responder_runs[]` (array of objects: `{comment_ids[], invoked_at, result}` — appended each time the responder runs).
+- Matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span.
+- `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession: write the successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded.
+**Gate:** an end-to-end `verify`-labelled ticket drains; new fields populate (all as `null` since no roles are wired yet); existing behaviour unchanged.
+### M2 — Worker-internal loop (Task Shaper, Outcome Verifier, halt path)

+- **Task Shaper** (`/shape:outcome-tasks`): refined skill that takes a Linear issue and produces enriched AC checklist + sizing decision (single stack vs N stacks) + per-stack task list. Open decision B in §10 governs whether this is a full reshape of `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` or a narrower variant alongside.
+- **Implementer integration**: worker session pulls the Task Shaper output as context; no other implementer changes.

+- **Outcome Verifier** (`/shape:outcome-verify`): new skill. Inputs: ticket body + Task Shaper output + diff + run-log. Output: structured verdict (pass / fail + findings) written to `outcome_verdict`.
+- **Halt-on-fail path**: extends decision §8 / §9 machinery — on Outcome Verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.
+**Gate:** 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate failure (e.g. a synthetic ticket whose AC the implementer can't satisfy).
+### M3 — PR-shape gate (PR Preparer)

+- **PR Preparer** (`/shape:pr-prepare`): new skill. Reads stack PRs via Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body on each; decides auto-merge vs human-review per the carve-out rule (small + no human review needed → auto-merge; otherwise → human-review).
+- **Auto-merge mechanism**: defaults to Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-equivalent). Open decision F in §10 covers the alternative (`gh pr merge --auto`).
+- Records decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry.
+**Gate:** 3 stacks emitted, all with structured What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels. PR-body assertion in `drain-cycle grade` passes on all three.
+### M4 — PR-feedback loop (PR Responder)
+- **PR Responder** (`/shape:pr-respond`): new skill. Reads new review comments on stack PRs via Graphite MCP; makes the fix in the right worktree; pushes to the stack; appends to `responder_runs[]`.

+- **Trigger model**: defaults to polling-at-drain-start (each `drain-cycle drain` invocation checks for un-addressed review comments before picking up new tickets, and addresses any found). Open decision D in §10 covers the alternatives (GitHub Action → Linear ticket; one-keystroke `drain-cycle respond <pr>`).
+- **Idempotency**: each comment ID tracked in `responder_runs[].comment_ids[]` so the same comment isn't addressed twice across drains.
+**Gate:** one real PR with operator-left review feedback gets addressed end-to-end by the responder; fix lands on the stack; run-log shows the responder run.
+### M5 — Grading + cost + close-out
+- **Per-ticket grade template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with checklist + `status:` field.

+- **`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand**: one-shot grading prompt. Invoked automatically on drain completion (writes `status: draft`); also invokable manually by the operator for retro-fill on past tickets.
+- **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand**: reads `confirmed` grade files, reports pass-rate across a ticket window, warns about un-confirmed drafts.
+- **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): exit-code-nonzero on any `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict == null` entry.
+- **Cost-impact handling**: measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
+- **Post-launch review**: write a Linear comment on each KR's status at end of the initiative window; either ship cleanly, pause, or kill.
+**Gate:** KR grading runs end-to-end; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} is decided and recorded.
## §8 Kill condition

-If, after running the new flow on **5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles**, the verifier produces **zero true-positive findings** (every verifier halt was a false positive, and verifier-passed Dones don't visibly improve PR-review outcomes vs default-flow drains) — the planner+verifier overhead isn't earning its keep.
+Two layers:

-Action on kill: revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields (`flow` and `verifier_verdict` stay in run log + OTel — they're cheap and useful for future investigation); record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.

+**Per-milestone gates as stop-the-line points.** Each M1–M5 gate is proceed-or-kill, not "review and continue." Failure at a gate triggers a kill/reshape decision before the next milestone starts. The clearest kill at each:
+- M1 — schema additions cause unanticipated breakage in existing run-log readers or downstream tooling.
+- M2 — 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles produce zero true-positive findings from Outcome Verifier (every halt was a false positive). Carried from the round-3 doc.
+- M3 — PR Preparer's bodies don't measurably reduce reviewer cognitive load (signal: operator subjectively prefers the raw worker-produced PRs).
+- M4 — PR Responder either (a) can't address comments cleanly without operator intervention >50% of the time, or (b) the trigger model produces dropped/missed comments.

+**Action on kill (any milestone):** revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields — they stay in run log + OTel because they're cheap and useful for future investigation; record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.
## §9 Out of scope (explicitly)
-- **PR feedback loop on stacked Graphite PRs** — sibling initiative. Shares skill-as-primitive philosophy but has its own trigger problem and blast radius.
-- **Parallel drains** — orthogonal; correctness-focused initiative has no reason to touch decision §8's serial-drain stance.
-- **Authoring new skills** in `agent-skills-shaper` — initiative reuses existing skills; new-skill authoring would be a separate initiative if needed.
+**Moves into scope (vs round-3):** PR Preparer (M3); PR Responder / feedback loop (M4).
-## §10 Open review points (round 3)
+**Stays out of scope:**
+- **Parallel drains.** Decision §8's serial-drain stance unchanged.
+- **Replacing Graphite.** The PR-side work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow; switching PR-management tools is a separate decision.
+- **Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows.** Single-user product per §3.
