content.content:
130	
131	Each KR's claim (its headline) should be one of: a binary pass/fail ("X works on the common path with no manual intervention"), a fitness function firing ("the guard fails the run loudly when Y"), or a measurable delta ("token footprint drops vs baseline"). No "improve X" / "better Y" / "run N times" language. Every KR must have all four sub-fields (baseline / target / measured over / how we'll know), a `(commit|stretch)` tag, and a `bet`/`brake`/`foundation` role appended to the headline. Add the audit-footer line (`*Layer 1: … · Layer 2: …*`) under each KR, and the dimensions-summary line after the KR list — this is the rubric trace, kept out of the KR body so the OKR reads cleanly to a non-pack-author.
132	
133	Present the draft. Do not create the Linear project yet.
134	
135	**6. [GATE] User confirms the draft.**
136	Ask explicitly: "Does this capture the initiative correctly? Any changes to the Objective, the Key results, kill condition, or project type before I create the project?" Do not proceed until confirmed. Fixing a wrong problem statement, vague KR, or missing kill condition here takes one minute; fixing it mid-cycle costs days.
137	
138	**6.5. [GATE] Verification rubric — 11 cross-cutting rules.**
139	Run the following checks against the confirmed draft. Each rule is a binary pass/fail. Any failure returns the draft to Step 5 for repair — the Linear project is NOT created until all 11 pass.
140	
141	| # | Rule | Pass condition |
142	|---|---|---|
143	| 1 | All six fields present | Goal / Key results / Affected repos / Appetite / Kill condition / Project type are all non-empty |
144	| 2 | Goal names who + outcome | Sentence contains an affected party and a desired outcome — no solution verb ("build", "add", "integrate", "ship") |
145	| 3 | Initiative name is a goal/problem | Name does not open with a solution verb ("Build X", "Add Y", "Integrate Z") |
146	| 4 | At least 3 KRs, no more than 5 | KR count ≥ 3 and ≤ 5 |
147	| 5a | No degenerate KRs | `target ≠ baseline` either numerically (different number) or by state change (different observable state) — bans "baseline 100%, target 100%" patterns |
148	| 5b | KRs name distinct Layer 1 dimensions | Across all KRs, no two share the same Layer 1 dimension. Default vocabulary: correctness / outcome / maintenance / discipline (see `references/kr-quality-templates.md`). An out-of-default dimension (e.g. performance) is permitted only when the initiative genuinely has that property |
149	| 5c | KRs are not Goal-restatements | Strip the Goal to its measurable noun-phrase; no KR's observable state matches it. Each KR must name a *measurable property of* the Goal — not the Goal itself |
150	| 5d | KR matches its Layer 2 template | For each KR, name which Layer 1 dimension it sits in and confirm it matches the template (golden-path test / self-trial protocol / structural cap + revisit gate / artefact-exists + sections-complete) in `references/kr-quality-templates.md` |
151	| 5e | Language ban | No KR contains "improve", "better", "enhance", or arbitrary "run N times" count thresholds without a correctness or quality dimension |
152	| 5f | KR is grader-backed (or carved out with justification) | Each KR's `how we'll know:` field points at a command / script / query that emits a verdict against the target — describable in one sentence at draft time. Manual grading is permitted only under the carve-out: (a) the initiative is genuinely one-shot or throwaway, OR (b) the verdict is irreducibly qualitative ("the prose reads naturally", "the diagram is legible") and no honest automatable proxy exists. The carve-out must be stated alongside the KR. Building the grader (write-side schema + read-side command) is scoped into the initiative as one or two user stories before it enters Active. See `references/kr-quality-templates.md` "The grader-backed KR pattern" |
153	| 6 | Every KR has all four sub-fields, no placeholders, `how we'll know` is a concrete artefact | Each KR has baseline, target, measured over, how we'll know — none blank, none equal to `TBD`, `unknown`, `currently unknown`, `we'll measure it`, `we'll figure it out`, `various`, `the logs`, `the system`, `the dashboard`, `better than today`, `the report`. `how we'll know` points to a concrete artefact: a file path, a directory, a Linear query, a log query (e.g. `bigquery: events WHERE ...`), a cached report URL, or a metric name + system |
154	| 7 | Every KR is tagged | Each KR carries exactly one of `(commit)` or `(stretch)` |
155	| 8 | Kill condition is present | Non-empty; names an observable state, not a vague clause ("if it doesn't work") |
156	| 9 | Project type is canonical | One of: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6 — not free text |
157	| 10 | Appetite is in issues | Expressed as "~N issues" — not days, weeks, or sprints |
158	| 11 | Every KR has a role tag | Each KR carries exactly one of `bet` / `brake` / `foundation` — roles defined in `references/kr-quality-templates.md` "Roles within an initiative" |
159	
160	**Soft check (warning, not a hard fail):** if every KR is tagged `bet`, surface a warning — most initiatives have at most 1–2 bets, and an all-bet draft usually means the brakes and foundation were silently omitted. This does not block project creation, but name it to the user and ask whether a brake (something that already works and shouldn't regress) or foundation (instrumentation the bet depends on to be measurable) KR is missing. Do not force one of each role — some shapes are honestly 2 bets + 1 foundation, or 1 bet + 2 brakes.
161	
162	Report each failing rule by number with the specific text that triggered the failure. For rule 5, name the specific sub-rule (5a / 5b / 5c / 5d / 5e / 5f) that failed, so the user knows whether to fix the baseline-equals-target problem (5a), the duplicate-dimension problem (5b), the Goal-restatement problem (5c), the template-mismatch problem (5d), the language problem (5e), or the missing-grader problem (5f). When all checks pass, proceed to Step 7.
163	
164	**7. Create the Linear project.**
165	Call `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_project` with:
166	- `name`: goal or problem label — not a solution name, not a repo name
167	- `description`: the six-field initiative format (see template below)
168	- Status: Planned
169	
170	Confirm the project URL and share it.
171	
172	**8. Optional: assign known issues.**
173	If the user listed existing issues for this initiative, list them and offer to reassign them to the new project via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_issue`. Assign only the ones the user confirms.
174	
175	## Initiative description template
176	
177	The output is the Linear project description. It must read as a product OKR — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each KR in 30 seconds. Each KR's headline *is* the observable claim (there is no separate `state:` field). The four sub-fields use PM-readable names; the rubric vocabulary lives only in the italic audit-footer under each KR and the dimensions-summary line after the list — never in the KR body. The headline ends with a PM-readable `*(bet|brake|foundation)*` role tag (the push / a guardrail / an enabler) — plain English, so it stays on the headline, unlike the Layer 1/Layer 2 rubric vocabulary that lives in the footer.
178	
179	```markdown
180	**Goal:** For [who], we want to [solve problem / achieve outcome].
181	
182	**Key results:**
183	
184	**KR1 (commit|stretch)** — [the observable claim as one readable sentence: binary pass/fail, fitness function firing, or measurable delta] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
185	- baseline: [current value/state]
186	- target:   [target value/state]
187	- measured over: [time frame or N units — "by cycle close", "next 10 listings", "within 30 days"]
188	- how we'll know: [where the evidence lives — file path / query / log / cached report; if grader-backed, the named file plus the one-sentence command that emits the verdict]
189	
190	*Layer 1: [dimension] · Layer 2: [template]*
191	
192	**KR2 (commit|stretch)** — [observable claim] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
193	- baseline: ...
194	- target:   ...
195	- measured over: ...
196	- how we'll know: ...
197	
198	*Layer 1: [dimension] · Layer 2: [template]*
199	
200	**KR3 (commit|stretch)** — [observable claim] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
201	- baseline: ...
202	- target:   ...
203	- measured over: ...
204	- how we'll know: ...

file.content:
Each KR's claim (its headline) should be one of: a binary pass/fail ("X works on the common path with no manual intervention"), a fitness function firing ("the guard fails the run loudly when Y"), or a measurable delta ("token footprint drops vs baseline"). No "improve X" / "better Y" / "run N times" language. Every KR must have all four sub-fields (baseline / target / measured over / how we'll know), a `(commit|stretch)` tag, and a `bet`/`brake`/`foundation` role appended to the headline. Add the audit-footer line (`*Layer 1: … · Layer 2: …*`) under each KR, and the dimensions-summary line after the KR list — this is the rubric trace, kept out of the KR body so the OKR reads cleanly to a non-pack-author.

Present the draft. Do not create the Linear project yet.

**6. [GATE] User confirms the draft.**
Ask explicitly: "Does this capture the initiative correctly? Any changes to the Objective, the Key results, kill condition, or project type before I create the project?" Do not proceed until confirmed. Fixing a wrong problem statement, vague KR, or missing kill condition here takes one minute; fixing it mid-cycle costs days.

**6.5. [GATE] Verification rubric — 11 cross-cutting rules.**
Run the following checks against the confirmed draft. Each rule is a binary pass/fail. Any failure returns the draft to Step 5 for repair — the Linear project is NOT created until all 11 pass.

| # | Rule | Pass condition |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | All six fields present | Goal / Key results / Affected repos / Appetite / Kill condition / Project type are all non-empty |
| 2 | Goal names who + outcome | Sentence contains an affected party and a desired outcome — no solution verb ("build", "add", "integrate", "ship") |
| 3 | Initiative name is a goal/problem | Name does not open with a solution verb ("Build X", "Add Y", "Integrate Z") |
| 4 | At least 3 KRs, no more than 5 | KR count ≥ 3 and ≤ 5 |
| 5a | No degenerate KRs | `target ≠ baseline` either numerically (different number) or by state change (different observable state) — bans "baseline 100%, target 100%" patterns |
| 5b | KRs name distinct Layer 1 dimensions | Across all KRs, no two share the same Layer 1 dimension. Default vocabulary: correctness / outcome / maintenance / discipline (see `references/kr-quality-templates.md`). An out-of-default dimension (e.g. performance) is permitted only when the initiative genuinely has that property |
| 5c | KRs are not Goal-restatements | Strip the Goal to its measurable noun-phrase; no KR's observable state matches it. Each KR must name a *measurable property of* the Goal — not the Goal itself |
| 5d | KR matches its Layer 2 template | For each KR, name which Layer 1 dimension it sits in and confirm it matches the template (golden-path test / self-trial protocol / structural cap + revisit gate / artefact-exists + sections-complete) in `references/kr-quality-templates.md` |
| 5e | Language ban | No KR contains "improve", "better", "enhance", or arbitrary "run N times" count thresholds without a correctness or quality dimension |
| 5f | KR is grader-backed (or carved out with justification) | Each KR's `how we'll know:` field points at a command / script / query that emits a verdict against the target — describable in one sentence at draft time. Manual grading is permitted only under the carve-out: (a) the initiative is genuinely one-shot or throwaway, OR (b) the verdict is irreducibly qualitative ("the prose reads naturally", "the diagram is legible") and no honest automatable proxy exists. The carve-out must be stated alongside the KR. Building the grader (write-side schema + read-side command) is scoped into the initiative as one or two user stories before it enters Active. See `references/kr-quality-templates.md` "The grader-backed KR pattern" |
| 6 | Every KR has all four sub-fields, no placeholders, `how we'll know` is a concrete artefact | Each KR has baseline, target, measured over, how we'll know — none blank, none equal to `TBD`, `unknown`, `currently unknown`, `we'll measure it`, `we'll figure it out`, `various`, `the logs`, `the system`, `the dashboard`, `better than today`, `the report`. `how we'll know` points to a concrete artefact: a file path, a directory, a Linear query, a log query (e.g. `bigquery: events WHERE ...`), a cached report URL, or a metric name + system |
| 7 | Every KR is tagged | Each KR carries exactly one of `(commit)` or `(stretch)` |
| 8 | Kill condition is present | Non-empty; names an observable state, not a vague clause ("if it doesn't work") |
| 9 | Project type is canonical | One of: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, or 6 — not free text |
| 10 | Appetite is in issues | Expressed as "~N issues" — not days, weeks, or sprints |
| 11 | Every KR has a role tag | Each KR carries exactly one of `bet` / `brake` / `foundation` — roles defined in `references/kr-quality-templates.md` "Roles within an initiative" |

**Soft check (warning, not a hard fail):** if every KR is tagged `bet`, surface a warning — most initiatives have at most 1–2 bets, and an all-bet draft usually means the brakes and foundation were silently omitted. This does not block project creation, but name it to the user and ask whether a brake (something that already works and shouldn't regress) or foundation (instrumentation the bet depends on to be measurable) KR is missing. Do not force one of each role — some shapes are honestly 2 bets + 1 foundation, or 1 bet + 2 brakes.

Report each failing rule by number with the specific text that triggered the failure. For rule 5, name the specific sub-rule (5a / 5b / 5c / 5d / 5e / 5f) that failed, so the user knows whether to fix the baseline-equals-target problem (5a), the duplicate-dimension problem (5b), the Goal-restatement problem (5c), the template-mismatch problem (5d), the language problem (5e), or the missing-grader problem (5f). When all checks pass, proceed to Step 7.

**7. Create the Linear project.**
Call `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_project` with:
- `name`: goal or problem label — not a solution name, not a repo name
- `description`: the six-field initiative format (see template below)
- Status: Planned

Confirm the project URL and share it.

**8. Optional: assign known issues.**
If the user listed existing issues for this initiative, list them and offer to reassign them to the new project via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_issue`. Assign only the ones the user confirms.

## Initiative description template

The output is the Linear project description. It must read as a product OKR — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each KR in 30 seconds. Each KR's headline *is* the observable claim (there is no separate `state:` field). The four sub-fields use PM-readable names; the rubric vocabulary lives only in the italic audit-footer under each KR and the dimensions-summary line after the list — never in the KR body. The headline ends with a PM-readable `*(bet|brake|foundation)*` role tag (the push / a guardrail / an enabler) — plain English, so it stays on the headline, unlike the Layer 1/Layer 2 rubric vocabulary that lives in the footer.

```markdown
**Goal:** For [who], we want to [solve problem / achieve outcome].

**Key results:**

**KR1 (commit|stretch)** — [the observable claim as one readable sentence: binary pass/fail, fitness function firing, or measurable delta] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
- baseline: [current value/state]
- target:   [target value/state]
- measured over: [time frame or N units — "by cycle close", "next 10 listings", "within 30 days"]
- how we'll know: [where the evidence lives — file path / query / log / cached report; if grader-backed, the named file plus the one-sentence command that emits the verdict]

*Layer 1: [dimension] · Layer 2: [template]*

**KR2 (commit|stretch)** — [observable claim] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
- baseline: ...
- target:   ...
- measured over: ...
- how we'll know: ...

*Layer 1: [dimension] · Layer 2: [template]*

**KR3 (commit|stretch)** — [observable claim] *(bet|brake|foundation)*
- baseline: ...
- target:   ...
- measured over: ...
- how we'll know: ...

*Layer 1: [dimension] · Layer 2: [template]*

*Dimensions: [d1] / [d2] / [d3] — all distinct per rule 5b.*

content.content:
1	# Draft Linear initiative — multi-agent collaboration for correctness
2	
3	**Status:** draft for review (round 4 reshape; Step 5 of `initiative-shape`). Linear project NOT created yet — pending operator confirmation (Step 6) and the 11-rule verification rubric (Step 6.5).
4	
5	**How to use this doc:** leave inline feedback on any section. The roster in §1, the KRs in §6, and the milestone structure in §7 are the primary review surfaces.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## §1 Raw idea (Step 1)
10	
11	drain-cycle evolves from "drain a Linear cycle unattended" toward a **UNIX-philosophy dispatcher for composable skill primitives** — Linear tickets as the work queue, skills+MCPs as the small tools, and `drain-cycle` itself as the batch-mode shell that composes them. Operator (you) drives the *why* (initiative goal + KRs) and the *what* (outcome-focused Linear issue); agents handle the *how* through a defined set of skills. The same skills are invokable manually at the keyboard OR automatically inside a worker session — design decision §10's symlink already makes this true.
12	
13	The operator failure mode this initiative addresses: **complex tickets that can't be sliced further** where the worker forgets details, under-verifies, or submits an unstructured PR — followed by human review comments that pile up while the operator manually re-invokes the agent to address each round.
14	
15	The bet: six agent-driven roles end-to-end, each backed by a skill (or CLI subcommand) that fits the UNIX-philosophy frame:
16	
17	| # | Role | Phase | Skill / CLI | Status |
18	|---|---|---|---|---|
19	| 0 | **Operator** (you) | Throughout | Operator-side skill table; defines *why* + *what* | — |
20	| 1 | **Task Shaper** | Before code | `/shape:outcome-tasks` *(refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`)* | [R] |
21	| 2 | **Implementer** | Middle | 6 existing skills (incremental-implementation, test-driven-development, source-driven-development, code-review-and-quality, git-workflow-and-versioning, debugging-and-error-recovery) | [E] |
22	| 3 | **Outcome Verifier** | After implementation, before Done | `/shape:outcome-verify` *(new)* | [N] |
23	| 4 | **PR Preparer** | After Outcome Verifier passes, before PR submission | `/shape:pr-prepare` *(new)* | [N] |
24	| 5 | **PR Responder** | On new human review comments (operator-launched polling loop) | `/shape:pr-respond` *(new)*, launched via `drain-cycle pr-feedback` subcommand | [N] |
25	| 6 | **Grader** (post-drain) | After worker session finishes | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | [—] |
26	
27	Roles 1 and 3 are a **complementary pair**: `/shape:outcome-tasks` defines the outcome-shaped task list at the start; `/shape:outcome-verify` validates the outcome at the end. Roles 4 and 5 are the **PR-side pair**: Preparer shapes outbound PRs, Responder addresses inbound review feedback. Role 6 is operator-confirm scaffolding for grading the initiative against its KRs.
28	
29	**Constraints carried from prior rounds:**
30	- Subscription auth only (Claude Code / Codex CLI subprocess) — no API-key models. GitHub webhooks themselves are free, but the agent itself can't run inside GitHub Actions; agent work has to happen on the operator's machine.
31	- Correctness > throughput. No parallel drains. No throughput-focused changes.
32	
33	## §2 Problem-or-solution gate (Step 2)
34	
35	**Passes.** The operator-impacting failures are named and specific:
36	
37	1. **Silent Done on hard tickets.** Complex unsliceable tickets get drained; worker self-asserts Done; produced diff misses AC; operator finds out at PR review and fixes by hand.
38	2. **Unstructured PR submission.** PRs land without What/Why/Focus guidance; reviewer has to guess what to look at, or the operator manually rewrites the body.
39	3. **Stalled feedback loops.** Human review comments accumulate on stacked PRs; operator manually re-invokes claude to address each round.
40	
41	The six-role roster is the proposed solution; each role maps to one of the failure modes (Task Shaper + Outcome Verifier address #1; PR Preparer addresses #2; PR Responder addresses #3).
42	
43	## §3 Project type (Step 2.5)
44	
45	**Type 2 — personal product, single-user.** Confirmed.
46	
47	Rationale: drain-cycle is described in `AGENTS.md` as "Python, single-user, personal product"; the artefact being evolved is the product itself; any new skills/MCPs are primitives drain-cycle invokes, not the headline deliverable; one user (you), so growth/MAU KRs are categorically inapplicable per Type 2 playbook.
48	
49	## §4 Negative outcome if not solved
50	
51	If the initiative is not built:
52	
53	- **Hard tickets continue to ship Done with missing AC.** The worker self-asserts Done (per design decision §1); the only feedback is at PR-review time, when the operator either accepts the gap or pushes manual fix commits. The unattended-drain promise breaks on exactly the tickets where it matters most.
54	- **PR review burden falls entirely on the human.** Reviewers (operator + collaborators) open PRs with no guidance on what to focus on; auto-mergeable changes get the same scrutiny as risky ones; review throughput is slower than it needs to be.
55	- **Human review feedback creates a manual re-invocation tax.** Each round of comments on a stacked PR requires the operator to context-switch back into the worktree, re-launch claude, and shepherd the agent through the fix. The agent ecosystem stops at code-submission time instead of running end-to-end through merge.
56	
57	## §5 Affected repos
58	
59	- **`drain-cycle`** — primary. Worker integration of roles 1–5, run-log schema extensions, `verify` label mechanism, CLI subcommands (`grade-draft`, `grade --flow=verify`), supersession of design decision §1, OTel attribute additions.
60	- **`agent-skills-shaper`** — secondary, conditional. Receives one refined skill (`/shape:outcome-tasks`, refined from `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) and three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`). Open decision C in §10 controls whether new-skill authoring happens inside this initiative or splits to a sibling.
61	
62	## §6 Goal and Key results
63	
64	The KRs are written in plain English — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each in 30 seconds. Rubric annotations (Layer 1 / Layer 2 dimensions) live in italic audit-footers under each KR.
65	
66	### §6.0 Goal
67	
68	**For** the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear tickets, **we want** six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done, structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review comments — **so that** complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC, PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't create a manual re-invocation tax.
69	
70	### §6.1 KR1 (stretch) — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow, ≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*
71	
72	- **plain-English version:** when I run drain-cycle on a hard ticket I couldn't break into smaller pieces, six agent roles take it from intake to merged PR. The bet is that this catches enough real problems and structures the PRs well enough that most merge cleanly without me having to push fix commits.
73	- **baseline:** 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today.
74	- **target:** ≥ 3 of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits.
75	- **measured over:** the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow (~2 cycles at current cadence).
76	- **how we'll grade it:** per-ticket grade markdown at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms `draft` → `confirmed`); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate across the window.
77	
78	*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*
79	
80	### §6.2 KR2 (commit) — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions *(brake)*
81	
82	- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows the Outcome Verifier ran and produced a verdict. Protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
83	- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
84	- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log.
85	- **measured over:** every cycle that runs the new flow, for the initiative's duration (cap at 10 cycles if the initiative runs long).
86	- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.
87	
88	*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*
89	
90	### §6.3 KR3 (commit) — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*
91	
92	- **plain-English version:** the PR Preparer either writes a structured What / Why / Focus body and routes the PR for human review, or it correctly identifies the PR as auto-mergeable (no critical focus items) and routes it to auto-merge. Either way, every PR comes out with a documented routing decision and a reviewable body.
93	- **baseline:** today, PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance.
94	- **target:** 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning attached.
95	- **measured over:** every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration.
96	- **how we'll grade it:** PR Preparer writes its decision to a `prep_verdict` field on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line.
97	
98	*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*
99	
100	### §6.4 KR rule check
101	
102	Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) passes: three KRs covering three distinct failure modes (correctness, maintenance discipline, PR-shape discipline). Rule 5b (distinct dimensions) passes: correctness / maintenance / discipline. No override needed.
103	
104	The PR Responder (role 5) doesn't get its own KR — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback). Adding a fourth KR for responder-specific metrics would push toward padding given KR1 already exercises that surface end-to-end.
105	
106	## §7 Appetite
107	
108	**~18–22 issues across five milestones.** Each milestone has a review gate at the end — the operator reviews progress and either continues to the next milestone or pauses for a reshape/kill decision if something clear has come up. Default at each gate is review-and-continue, not stop-the-line (per round-7 c_8ebf0b). Whole-initiative kill conditions are separate (§8).
109	
110	### M1 — Recording infrastructure
111	
112	Build the data layer first, behaviour later.
113	
114	- `verify` Linear label parser added alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution in `labels.py`.
115	- Run-log schema fields: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow), `outcome_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null`), `prep_verdict` (object: `{result, route, reasoning}` or `null`), `responder_runs[]` (array of objects: `{comment_ids[], invoked_at, result}` — appended each time the responder runs).
116	- Matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span.
117	- `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession: write the successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded.
118	
119	**Gate:** an end-to-end `verify`-labelled ticket drains; new fields populate (all as `null` since no roles are wired yet); existing behaviour unchanged.
120	
121	### M2 — Worker-internal loop (Task Shaper, Outcome Verifier, halt path)
122	
123	- **Task Shaper** (`/shape:outcome-tasks`): refined skill that takes a Linear issue and produces enriched AC checklist + sizing decision (single stack vs N stacks) + per-stack task list. Open decision B in §10 governs whether this is a full reshape of `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` or a narrower variant alongside.
124	- **Where the sizing decision lands** (per round-7 c_3a5c84): dual-write. (a) Fed into the implementer in-session as context; (b) appended to the **Linear issue body** so future resumes / pickups have the full context without re-running the Task Shaper. The Linear write is the durable artefact.
125	- **Implementer integration**: worker session pulls the Task Shaper output as context; no other implementer changes.
126	- **Outcome Verifier** (`/shape:outcome-verify`): new skill. Inputs: ticket body + Task Shaper output + diff + run-log. Output: structured verdict (pass / fail + findings) written to `outcome_verdict`.
127	- **Halt-on-fail path**: extends decision §8 / §9 machinery — on Outcome Verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.
128	
129	**Gate:** 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate failure (e.g. a synthetic ticket whose AC the implementer can't satisfy).
130	
131	### M3 — PR-shape gate (PR Preparer)
132	
133	- **PR Preparer** (`/shape:pr-prepare`): new skill. Reads stack PRs via Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body on each; decides auto-merge vs human-review per the carve-out rule (small + no human review needed → auto-merge; otherwise → human-review).
134	- **Auto-merge mechanism**: defaults to Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-equivalent). Open decision F in §10 covers the alternative (`gh pr merge --auto`).
135	- Records decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry.
136	
137	**Gate:** 3 stacks emitted, all with structured What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels. PR-body assertion in `drain-cycle grade` passes on all three.
138	
139	### M4 — PR-feedback loop (PR Responder)
140	
141	- **PR Responder** (`/shape:pr-respond`): new skill. Reads new review comments on stack PRs via Graphite MCP; makes the fix in the right worktree; pushes to the stack; appends to `responder_runs[]`.
142	- **Trigger model**: a separate operator-launched subcommand — `drain-cycle pr-feedback` (per rp_c4bc10). The operator launches it explicitly; the command then runs as a polling loop, addressing PR comments as they arrive, until the operator stops it. Not invoked implicitly at drain-start, not a GitHub Action, not background-daemon-on-boot. Foreground polling agent the operator owns the lifetime of.
143	- **Idempotency**: each comment ID tracked in `responder_runs[].comment_ids[]` so the same comment isn't addressed twice across `pr-feedback` invocations.
144	
145	**Gate:** one real PR with operator-left review feedback gets addressed end-to-end by the responder; fix lands on the stack; run-log shows the responder run.
146	
147	### M5 — Grading + cost + close-out
148	
149	- **Per-ticket grade template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with checklist + `status:` field.
150	- **`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand**: one-shot grading prompt. Invoked automatically on drain completion (writes `status: draft`); also invokable manually by the operator for retro-fill on past tickets.
151	- **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand**: reads `confirmed` grade files, reports pass-rate across a ticket window, warns about un-confirmed drafts.
152	- **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): exit-code-nonzero on any `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict == null` entry.
153	- **Cost-impact handling**: measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
154	- **Post-launch review**: write a Linear comment on each KR's status at end of the initiative window; either ship cleanly, pause, or kill.
155	
156	**Gate:** KR grading runs end-to-end; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} is decided and recorded.
157	
158	## §8 Kill condition
159	
160	**Primary kill (whole-initiative):** after running the new flow on 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles, the Outcome Verifier produces zero true-positive findings (every halt was a false positive, and verifier-passed Dones don't visibly improve PR-review outcomes vs default-flow drains). Carried from round-3.
161	
162	**Secondary concerns (watched at each milestone's review gate, not auto-kills per round-7 c_8ebf0b):**
163	
164	- M1 — schema additions cause unanticipated breakage in existing run-log readers or downstream tooling.
165	- M3 — PR Preparer's bodies don't measurably reduce reviewer cognitive load (signal: operator subjectively prefers the raw worker-produced PRs).
166	- M4 — PR Responder either (a) can't address comments cleanly without operator intervention >50% of the time, or (b) the polling loop drops or misses comments in practice.
167	
168	Any of these surfacing at a milestone review prompts a discussion (continue / reshape / kill), but the default is continue. Only the primary kill above is automatic.
169	
170	**Action on kill:** revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields — they stay in run log + OTel because they're cheap and useful for future investigation; record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.
171	
172	## §9 Out of scope (explicitly)
173	
174	**Moves into scope (vs round-3):** PR Preparer (M3); PR Responder / feedback loop (M4).
175	
176	**Stays out of scope:**
177	- **Parallel drains.** Decision §8's serial-drain stance unchanged.
178	- **Replacing Graphite.** The PR-side work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow; switching PR-management tools is a separate decision.
179	- **Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows.** Single-user product per §3.
180	
181	## §10 Open review points (round 4)
182	
183	**Resolved in rounds 1–3:**
184	- ~~§6 KRs need plain English~~ [r1, c_7918fa]
185	- ~~§6.1 clarify what's new (metrics/logs?)~~ [r1, c_b1b472]

# Draft Linear initiative — multi-agent collaboration for correctness

**Status:** draft for review (round 4 reshape; Step 5 of `initiative-shape`). Linear project NOT created yet — pending operator confirmation (Step 6) and the 11-rule verification rubric (Step 6.5).

**How to use this doc:** leave inline feedback on any section. The roster in §1, the KRs in §6, and the milestone structure in §7 are the primary review surfaces.

---

## §1 Raw idea (Step 1)

drain-cycle evolves from "drain a Linear cycle unattended" toward a **UNIX-philosophy dispatcher for composable skill primitives** — Linear tickets as the work queue, skills+MCPs as the small tools, and `drain-cycle` itself as the batch-mode shell that composes them. Operator (you) drives the *why* (initiative goal + KRs) and the *what* (outcome-focused Linear issue); agents handle the *how* through a defined set of skills. The same skills are invokable manually at the keyboard OR automatically inside a worker session — design decision §10's symlink already makes this true.

The operator failure mode this initiative addresses: **complex tickets that can't be sliced further** where the worker forgets details, under-verifies, or submits an unstructured PR — followed by human review comments that pile up while the operator manually re-invokes the agent to address each round.

The bet: six agent-driven roles end-to-end, each backed by a skill (or CLI subcommand) that fits the UNIX-philosophy frame:

| # | Role | Phase | Skill / CLI | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | **Operator** (you) | Throughout | Operator-side skill table; defines *why* + *what* | — |
| 1 | **Task Shaper** | Before code | `/shape:outcome-tasks` *(refined `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`)* | [R] |
| 2 | **Implementer** | Middle | 6 existing skills (incremental-implementation, test-driven-development, source-driven-development, code-review-and-quality, git-workflow-and-versioning, debugging-and-error-recovery) | [E] |
| 3 | **Outcome Verifier** | After implementation, before Done | `/shape:outcome-verify` *(new)* | [N] |
| 4 | **PR Preparer** | After Outcome Verifier passes, before PR submission | `/shape:pr-prepare` *(new)* | [N] |
| 5 | **PR Responder** | On new human review comments (operator-launched polling loop) | `/shape:pr-respond` *(new)*, launched via `drain-cycle pr-feedback` subcommand | [N] |
| 6 | **Grader** (post-drain) | After worker session finishes | `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand | [—] |

Roles 1 and 3 are a **complementary pair**: `/shape:outcome-tasks` defines the outcome-shaped task list at the start; `/shape:outcome-verify` validates the outcome at the end. Roles 4 and 5 are the **PR-side pair**: Preparer shapes outbound PRs, Responder addresses inbound review feedback. Role 6 is operator-confirm scaffolding for grading the initiative against its KRs.

**Constraints carried from prior rounds:**
- Subscription auth only (Claude Code / Codex CLI subprocess) — no API-key models. GitHub webhooks themselves are free, but the agent itself can't run inside GitHub Actions; agent work has to happen on the operator's machine.
- Correctness > throughput. No parallel drains. No throughput-focused changes.

## §2 Problem-or-solution gate (Step 2)

**Passes.** The operator-impacting failures are named and specific:

1. **Silent Done on hard tickets.** Complex unsliceable tickets get drained; worker self-asserts Done; produced diff misses AC; operator finds out at PR review and fixes by hand.
2. **Unstructured PR submission.** PRs land without What/Why/Focus guidance; reviewer has to guess what to look at, or the operator manually rewrites the body.
3. **Stalled feedback loops.** Human review comments accumulate on stacked PRs; operator manually re-invokes claude to address each round.

The six-role roster is the proposed solution; each role maps to one of the failure modes (Task Shaper + Outcome Verifier address #1; PR Preparer addresses #2; PR Responder addresses #3).

## §3 Project type (Step 2.5)

**Type 2 — personal product, single-user.** Confirmed.

Rationale: drain-cycle is described in `AGENTS.md` as "Python, single-user, personal product"; the artefact being evolved is the product itself; any new skills/MCPs are primitives drain-cycle invokes, not the headline deliverable; one user (you), so growth/MAU KRs are categorically inapplicable per Type 2 playbook.

## §4 Negative outcome if not solved

If the initiative is not built:

- **Hard tickets continue to ship Done with missing AC.** The worker self-asserts Done (per design decision §1); the only feedback is at PR-review time, when the operator either accepts the gap or pushes manual fix commits. The unattended-drain promise breaks on exactly the tickets where it matters most.
- **PR review burden falls entirely on the human.** Reviewers (operator + collaborators) open PRs with no guidance on what to focus on; auto-mergeable changes get the same scrutiny as risky ones; review throughput is slower than it needs to be.
- **Human review feedback creates a manual re-invocation tax.** Each round of comments on a stacked PR requires the operator to context-switch back into the worktree, re-launch claude, and shepherd the agent through the fix. The agent ecosystem stops at code-submission time instead of running end-to-end through merge.

## §5 Affected repos

- **`drain-cycle`** — primary. Worker integration of roles 1–5, run-log schema extensions, `verify` label mechanism, CLI subcommands (`grade-draft`, `grade --flow=verify`), supersession of design decision §1, OTel attribute additions.
- **`agent-skills-shaper`** — secondary, conditional. Receives one refined skill (`/shape:outcome-tasks`, refined from `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown`) and three new skills (`/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond`). Open decision C in §10 controls whether new-skill authoring happens inside this initiative or splits to a sibling.

## §6 Goal and Key results

The KRs are written in plain English — a non-pack-author should be able to grade each in 30 seconds. Rubric annotations (Layer 1 / Layer 2 dimensions) live in italic audit-footers under each KR.

### §6.0 Goal

**For** the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear tickets, **we want** six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done, structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review comments — **so that** complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC, PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't create a manual re-invocation tax.

### §6.1 KR1 (stretch) — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow, ≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*

- **plain-English version:** when I run drain-cycle on a hard ticket I couldn't break into smaller pieces, six agent roles take it from intake to merged PR. The bet is that this catches enough real problems and structures the PRs well enough that most merge cleanly without me having to push fix commits.
- **baseline:** 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today.
- **target:** ≥ 3 of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits.
- **measured over:** the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow (~2 cycles at current cadence).
- **how we'll grade it:** per-ticket grade markdown at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms `draft` → `confirmed`); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate across the window.

*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*

### §6.2 KR2 (commit) — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the worker is no longer trusted to mark its own ticket Done. The contract is: a ticket using the new flow gets marked Done only if the run log shows the Outcome Verifier ran and produced a verdict. Protects against the failure mode where the agent thinks it's finished but actually missed half the AC.
- **baseline:** silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); count of past silent Dones is unknown because the recording fields don't exist yet.
- **target:** 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log.
- **measured over:** every cycle that runs the new flow, for the initiative's duration (cap at 10 cycles if the initiative runs long).
- **how we'll grade it:** `drain-cycle grade` is extended to fail any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent. Exit code ≠ 0 on any violation. A single violation in any cycle fails the KR — this is a brake, not a rate.

*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*

### §6.3 KR3 (commit) — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*

- **plain-English version:** the PR Preparer either writes a structured What / Why / Focus body and routes the PR for human review, or it correctly identifies the PR as auto-mergeable (no critical focus items) and routes it to auto-merge. Either way, every PR comes out with a documented routing decision and a reviewable body.
- **baseline:** today, PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance.
- **target:** 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning attached.
- **measured over:** every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration.
- **how we'll grade it:** PR Preparer writes its decision to a `prep_verdict` field on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line.

*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*

### §6.4 KR rule check

Rule 4 (≥ 3 KRs) passes: three KRs covering three distinct failure modes (correctness, maintenance discipline, PR-shape discipline). Rule 5b (distinct dimensions) passes: correctness / maintenance / discipline. No override needed.

The PR Responder (role 5) doesn't get its own KR — its existence is structurally captured by KR1 (clean-merge rate would degrade if the responder doesn't address review feedback). Adding a fourth KR for responder-specific metrics would push toward padding given KR1 already exercises that surface end-to-end.

## §7 Appetite

**~18–22 issues across five milestones.** Each milestone has a review gate at the end — the operator reviews progress and either continues to the next milestone or pauses for a reshape/kill decision if something clear has come up. Default at each gate is review-and-continue, not stop-the-line (per round-7 c_8ebf0b). Whole-initiative kill conditions are separate (§8).

### M1 — Recording infrastructure

Build the data layer first, behaviour later.

- `verify` Linear label parser added alongside the existing `repo:` / `model:` resolution in `labels.py`.
- Run-log schema fields: `flow` (string, e.g. `"verify"`, or `null` for default flow), `outcome_verdict` (object: `{result, findings[], invoked_at}` or `null`), `prep_verdict` (object: `{result, route, reasoning}` or `null`), `responder_runs[]` (array of objects: `{comment_ids[], invoked_at, result}` — appended each time the responder runs).
- Matching attributes on the `drain.issue` OTel span.
- `docs/design-decisions.md` §1 supersession: write the successor decision explaining the verifier-gated Done contract; mark §1 as superseded.

**Gate:** an end-to-end `verify`-labelled ticket drains; new fields populate (all as `null` since no roles are wired yet); existing behaviour unchanged.

### M2 — Worker-internal loop (Task Shaper, Outcome Verifier, halt path)

- **Task Shaper** (`/shape:outcome-tasks`): refined skill that takes a Linear issue and produces enriched AC checklist + sizing decision (single stack vs N stacks) + per-stack task list. Open decision B in §10 governs whether this is a full reshape of `/shape:planning-and-task-breakdown` or a narrower variant alongside.
- **Where the sizing decision lands** (per round-7 c_3a5c84): dual-write. (a) Fed into the implementer in-session as context; (b) appended to the **Linear issue body** so future resumes / pickups have the full context without re-running the Task Shaper. The Linear write is the durable artefact.
- **Implementer integration**: worker session pulls the Task Shaper output as context; no other implementer changes.
- **Outcome Verifier** (`/shape:outcome-verify`): new skill. Inputs: ticket body + Task Shaper output + diff + run-log. Output: structured verdict (pass / fail + findings) written to `outcome_verdict`.
- **Halt-on-fail path**: extends decision §8 / §9 machinery — on Outcome Verifier fail, leave worktree, halt cycle, name the breach in `cycle_halt_reason`.

**Gate:** 3 verify-labelled hard tickets drained; verdicts recorded; halt path exercised at least once on a deliberate failure (e.g. a synthetic ticket whose AC the implementer can't satisfy).

### M3 — PR-shape gate (PR Preparer)

- **PR Preparer** (`/shape:pr-prepare`): new skill. Reads stack PRs via Graphite MCP; writes What / Why / Focus body on each; decides auto-merge vs human-review per the carve-out rule (small + no human review needed → auto-merge; otherwise → human-review).
- **Auto-merge mechanism**: defaults to Graphite-native (`gt submit --merge-when-ready`-equivalent). Open decision F in §10 covers the alternative (`gh pr merge --auto`).
- Records decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry.

**Gate:** 3 stacks emitted, all with structured What / Why / Focus bodies or auto-merge labels. PR-body assertion in `drain-cycle grade` passes on all three.

### M4 — PR-feedback loop (PR Responder)

- **PR Responder** (`/shape:pr-respond`): new skill. Reads new review comments on stack PRs via Graphite MCP; makes the fix in the right worktree; pushes to the stack; appends to `responder_runs[]`.
- **Trigger model**: a separate operator-launched subcommand — `drain-cycle pr-feedback` (per rp_c4bc10). The operator launches it explicitly; the command then runs as a polling loop, addressing PR comments as they arrive, until the operator stops it. Not invoked implicitly at drain-start, not a GitHub Action, not background-daemon-on-boot. Foreground polling agent the operator owns the lifetime of.
- **Idempotency**: each comment ID tracked in `responder_runs[].comment_ids[]` so the same comment isn't addressed twice across `pr-feedback` invocations.

**Gate:** one real PR with operator-left review feedback gets addressed end-to-end by the responder; fix lands on the stack; run-log shows the responder run.

### M5 — Grading + cost + close-out

- **Per-ticket grade template** at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` with checklist + `status:` field.
- **`drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>` CLI subcommand**: one-shot grading prompt. Invoked automatically on drain completion (writes `status: draft`); also invokable manually by the operator for retro-fill on past tickets.
- **`drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` subcommand**: reads `confirmed` grade files, reports pass-rate across a ticket window, warns about un-confirmed drafts.
- **Silent-Done detection** (KR2 grader): exit-code-nonzero on any `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict == null` entry.
- **Cost-impact handling**: measure per-ticket token multiplier under the new flow; add `verify`-flow-specific overrides in `limits.yml` if cycle cap pressure shows up.
- **Post-launch review**: write a Linear comment on each KR's status at end of the initiative window; either ship cleanly, pause, or kill.

**Gate:** KR grading runs end-to-end; one of {ship cleanly, pause, kill} is decided and recorded.

## §8 Kill condition

**Primary kill (whole-initiative):** after running the new flow on 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles, the Outcome Verifier produces zero true-positive findings (every halt was a false positive, and verifier-passed Dones don't visibly improve PR-review outcomes vs default-flow drains). Carried from round-3.

**Secondary concerns (watched at each milestone's review gate, not auto-kills per round-7 c_8ebf0b):**

- M1 — schema additions cause unanticipated breakage in existing run-log readers or downstream tooling.
- M3 — PR Preparer's bodies don't measurably reduce reviewer cognitive load (signal: operator subjectively prefers the raw worker-produced PRs).
- M4 — PR Responder either (a) can't address comments cleanly without operator intervention >50% of the time, or (b) the polling loop drops or misses comments in practice.

Any of these surfacing at a milestone review prompts a discussion (continue / reshape / kill), but the default is continue. Only the primary kill above is automatic.

**Action on kill:** revert the contract enforcement (the `verify` label becomes a no-op or is removed); keep the recording fields — they stay in run log + OTel because they're cheap and useful for future investigation; record the negative result as a new decision in `docs/design-decisions.md`.

## §9 Out of scope (explicitly)

**Moves into scope (vs round-3):** PR Preparer (M3); PR Responder / feedback loop (M4).

**Stays out of scope:**
- **Parallel drains.** Decision §8's serial-drain stance unchanged.
- **Replacing Graphite.** The PR-side work assumes the existing Graphite stack flow; switching PR-management tools is a separate decision.
- **Multi-operator / multi-tenant workflows.** Single-user product per §3.

## §10 Open review points (round 4)

**Resolved in rounds 1–3:**
- ~~§6 KRs need plain English~~ [r1, c_7918fa]
- ~~§6.1 clarify what's new (metrics/logs?)~~ [r1, c_b1b472]
- ~~§6.1 single-trial too weak; need template for multi-ticket grading~~ [r1, c_d29df7]
- ~~§6.3 KR3 redundant with KR1; offered override~~ [r1, c_a2a5ee] *(re-introduced as PR-structure KR in round 4 with new scope)*
- ~~§7 slice 3 `flow:` label premature abstraction~~ [r1, c_dacf16]
- ~~§7 slice 1 — which skill for the planner spike?~~ [r2, c_befa07] *(planner spike dropped entirely in round 4)*
- ~~§7 slice 10 — grading template friction~~ [r2, c_1d55a4]
- ~~Roster names → skill names alignment~~ [plan-r2, c_7e2295]
- ~~Pre-Implementation Reviewer skill identity~~ [plan-r1, c_b76ab7] → resolved as `/shape:outcome-tasks`
- ~~Outcome Verifier complementary naming~~ [plan-r1, c_a406e2] → `/shape:outcome-verify`
- ~~Grader as skill vs CLI~~ [plan-r1, c_832b0f] → CLI, not skill

**Resolved in round 5:**
- ~~C — three-new-skill budget~~ [c_093b1b] → confirmed; `/shape:outcome-verify`, `/shape:pr-prepare`, `/shape:pr-respond` all stay in this initiative.
- ~~E — Task Shaper sizing rule~~ [c_df9c84] → agent-judged + reasoning logged. No numeric threshold guardrail.
- ~~F — auto-merge mechanism~~ [c_d4673d] → Graphite-native.

**Resolved in round 6:**
- ~~A — `/shape:outcome-tasks` rename adoption~~ [c_f2df6a] → adopt the rename.
- ~~D — PR Responder trigger model~~ [c_2fc556 + rp_c4bc10] → operator-launched polling subcommand `drain-cycle pr-feedback`. Operator launches it; it loops addressing PR comments until stopped.
- ~~G — KR3 commit-vs-stretch~~ [c_b614b5] → confirmed as commit/brake.

**Resolved in round 7:**
- ~~H — where the sizing decision lands~~ [c_3a5c84] → dual-write: fed into the implementer in-session AND appended to the Linear issue body for future resumes. Linear write is the durable artefact. §7 M2 updated.
- ~~I — milestone gates as kill points~~ [c_8ebf0b] → review-and-continue, not stop-the-line. §7 and §8 updated; primary kill (whole-initiative, 5-tickets-zero-true-positives) is the only automatic kill.

**Open for round 8+:**

B. **Refinement style for `/shape:outcome-tasks`** — full reshape of the existing skill (deprecating the general-purpose behaviour), or a narrower variant alongside? Flag for M2 design doc; doesn't need answering now.

## §11 Next steps

1. You leave inline feedback on this doc.
2. I revise affected sections.
3. Repeat until you confirm (Step 6 gate).
4. I run the 11-rule verification rubric (Step 6.5) and report any failures. Rule 4 should pass on its own (3 KRs); no override needed.
5. Once all 11 rules pass, I create the Linear project via `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_project` with the synthesised body, status Planned.
6. Sibling work (M1 design doc) becomes the next phase.

## Step 6.5 — 11-rule verification rubric

Walking the approved draft against the rubric in `initiative-shape/SKILL.md`:

| # | Rule | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | All six fields present | ✅ | Goal §6.0 · KRs §6.1–3 · Affected repos §5 · Appetite §7 · Kill condition §8 · Project type §3 |
| 2 | Goal names who + outcome, no solution verb | ✅ | "For the operator… *so that* complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC…" — no `build/add/integrate/ship` |
| 3 | Initiative name is a goal/problem | ✅ | "multi-agent collaboration for correctness" — noun phrase + outcome, not solution-verb |
| 4 | 3 ≤ KRs ≤ 5 | ✅ | Three KRs; no override needed |
| 5a | No degenerate KRs (target ≠ baseline) | ✅ | KR1 0/0 → ≥3/5; KR2 silent-self-assertion → zero; KR3 unstructured → structured-or-auto-merge |
| 5b | Distinct Layer 1 dimensions | ✅ | correctness · maintenance · discipline |
| 5c | KRs are not Goal-restatements | ✅ | Each KR is a measurable property of the goal (merge-clean rate / verdict presence / PR-body presence), not the goal itself |
| 5d | KR matches its Layer 2 template | ✅ | KR1 golden-path test · KR2 structural cap + revisit gate · KR3 artefact-exists + sections-complete |
| 5e | Language ban | ✅ | No `improve / better / enhance / run N times`; KR1's "next 5" is paired with a correctness verdict |
| 5f | KR is grader-backed | ✅ | KR1 → `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` over `~/.drain-cycle/grades/*.md`; KR2 → `drain-cycle grade` silent-Done check; KR3 → `prep_verdict` field check. Grader scaffolding scoped into M5 |
| 6 | Four sub-fields, no placeholders, `how we'll know` is concrete | ✅ | All sub-fields populated with concrete artefacts (file paths, run-log fields, CLI subcommands). KR2's "count of past silent Dones is unknown" is a stated *why-no-numeric-baseline*, not a `TBD` placeholder; the state baseline ("silent self-assertion currently possible") is concrete |
| 7 | Every KR has `(commit)` or `(stretch)` | ✅ | KR1 stretch · KR2 commit · KR3 commit |
| 8 | Kill condition observable | ✅ | "5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles, Outcome Verifier produces zero true-positive findings" |
| 9 | Project type canonical | ✅ | Type 2 |
| 10 | Appetite in issues | ✅ | `~18–22 issues across five milestones` |
| 11 | Every KR has role tag | ✅ | KR1 bet · KR2 brake · KR3 brake |

**Soft check (all-bet warning):** tags are bet / brake / brake — mixed, no warning fires.

**Result: 11/11 pass.** No repair pass needed.

---

## Step 7 — proposed Linear project body

Synthesised against the template in §"Initiative description template" of the skill. Before I call `mcp__claude_ai_Linear__save_project`, here is the exact `description` I'd write — please scan for last-minute edits:

```markdown
**Goal:** For the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear
tickets, we want six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to
merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done,
structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review
comments — so that complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC,
PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't
create a manual re-invocation tax.

**Key results:**

**KR1 (stretch)** — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow,
≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*
- baseline: 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today
- target: ≥ 3 of 5 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits
- measured over: the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow
  (~2 cycles at current cadence)
- how we'll know: per-ticket grade markdown at
  `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by
  `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms draft → confirmed);
  `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate.

*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*

**KR2 (commit)** — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow
has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions
*(brake)*
- baseline: silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision
  §1); past count unknown because recording fields don't exist yet
- target: 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an
  `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log
- measured over: every cycle that runs the new flow, capped at 10 cycles
- how we'll know: `drain-cycle grade` fails any entry where
  `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND
  `outcome_verdict` is absent; exit code ≠ 0 on any violation

*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*

**KR3 (commit)** — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured
What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*
- baseline: PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker
  produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance
- target: 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured
  What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning

*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*

for one refined skill + three new skills).

M2 worker-internal loop · M3 PR-shape gate · M4 PR-feedback loop ·
M5 grading + cost + close-out). Each milestone has a review-and-continue
gate; primary kill is whole-initiative only.

2 cycles, the Outcome Verifier produces zero true-positive findings
(every halt was a false positive, and verifier-passed Dones don't
visibly improve PR-review outcomes vs default-flow drains). On kill:
revert contract enforcement, keep recording fields, record negative
result in `docs/design-decisions.md`.

input.summary: Six agent-driven roles take hard tickets from intake to merged PR — task-shaping, outcome verification, structured PR prep, and autonomous PR-feedback response.
input.description:
**Goal:** For the operator running drain-cycle on outcome-focused Linear tickets, we want six agent-driven roles that take work from ticket to merged PR — task-shaping before code, outcome verification before Done, structured PR preparation, and autonomous response to human review comments — so that complex tickets don't silently land with missing AC, PRs are reviewable without guesswork, and human review feedback doesn't create a manual re-invocation tax.

**Key results:**

**KR1 (stretch)** — Of the next 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow, ≥ 3 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits *(bet)*
- baseline: 0 of 0 — none of the six roles exist as a connected flow today
- target: ≥ 3 of 5 ship and merge with no operator-pushed fix commits
- measured over: the first 5 hard tickets drained with the new flow (~2 cycles at current cadence)
- how we'll know: per-ticket grade markdown at `~/.drain-cycle/grades/<issue>.md` (auto-drafted by `drain-cycle grade-draft <issue>`, operator confirms draft → confirmed); `drain-cycle grade --flow=verify` reports the rolled-up pass-rate.

*Layer 1: correctness · Layer 2: golden-path test*

**KR2 (commit)** — Every ticket reaching Done in Linear under the new flow has a recorded Outcome Verifier verdict in the run log; zero exceptions *(brake)*
- baseline: silent self-assertion currently possible (per design decision §1); past count unknown because recording fields don't exist yet
- target: 0 tickets reach Done in Linear under the new flow without an `outcome_verdict` recorded in the run log
- measured over: every cycle that runs the new flow, capped at 10 cycles
- how we'll know: `drain-cycle grade` fails any entry where `flow == "verify"` AND `final_linear_state == "completed"` AND `outcome_verdict` is absent; exit code ≠ 0 on any violation

*Layer 1: maintenance · Layer 2: structural cap + revisit gate*

**KR3 (commit)** — Every PR submitted under the new flow has the structured What / Why / Focus body, or is correctly routed to auto-merge *(brake)*
- baseline: PRs from drained tickets have whatever body the worker produced — typically a one-line subject and no review guidance
- target: 0 PRs submitted under the new flow without either (a) structured What/Why/Focus body OR (b) auto-merge label with PR Preparer reasoning
- measured over: every PR submitted under the new flow, for the initiative's duration
- how we'll know: PR Preparer writes its decision to `prep_verdict` on the run-log entry; `drain-cycle grade` fails any verify-flow entry where `prep_verdict` is absent or where the verdict says "structured body" but the actual PR body is empty/single-line

*Layer 1: discipline · Layer 2: artefact-exists + sections-complete*

*Dimensions: correctness / maintenance / discipline — all distinct per rule 5b.*

**Kill condition:** after running the new flow on 5+ hard tickets across 2 cycles, the Outcome Verifier produces zero true-positive findings (every halt was a false positive, and verifier-passed Dones don't visibly improve PR-review outcomes vs default-flow drains). On kill: revert contract enforcement, keep recording fields, record negative result in `docs/design-decisions.md`.
